Faster than 1.2s → padded; slower → loading until bootstrap returns. 1.2s long enough to register as intentional UX beat, short enough to not be friction. Spec 'Visual treatment': Reuse existing loading page; no redesign. Message text may update to reflect restoration ('Restoring sessions…'); cosmetic and local to TUI. Spec 'Fatal Bootstrap Errors': TUI loading page never 'hangs forever.' Unrecoverable error tears down Bubble Tea cleanly. Loading kept up only while bootstrap making progress. Existing TUI model: lines 90-94 old messages, 144-145 old fields, 574-580 old poller, 607-635 Init scheduling, 687-701 Update handlers, 1267-1279 viewLoading.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Loading-Page Minimum Display (TUI Only), Bootstrap Flow → Return-to-Caller Timing → TUI path, Observability → Fatal Bootstrap Errors.

title: Dismiss TUI loading page on orchestrator completion via BootstrapCompleteMsg
description:
**Problem**: Task 5-6 landed TUI plumbing (minElapsed, bootstrapComplete fields, LoadingMinElapsedMsg tick, viewLoading copy) but bootstrapComplete never set — loading page hangs forever unless test flips manually. Spec says page dismisses when minElapsed && bootstrapComplete. bootstrapComplete must be set when bootstrap orchestrator (task 5-2) returns. Wires cmd/open.go's openTUI path. Because PersistentPreRunE has already run synchronously BEFORE openTUI, bootstrap is effectively 'complete' at openTUI entry. Simplest correct wiring: TUI emits BootstrapCompleteMsg from its own Init alongside other Init commands.

**Solution**: Init schedules fetchSessions + loadProjects + loadingPadTick + (new) `bootstrapCompleteCmd := func() tea.Msg { return BootstrapCompleteMsg{} }`. Update handles BootstrapCompleteMsg by setting m.bootstrapComplete=true + transitioning iff m.minElapsed. Because PersistentPreRunE completes synchronously before tea.NewProgram.Run() starts, orchestrator cannot fail AFTER TUI is running — wiring safe with no race window.

**Outcome**: TUI loading page appears, stays exactly 1.2s on fast bootstrap (padded), bootstrap-duration on slow (natural), dismisses cleanly. Decoupled from ListSessions rows — empty saved state still dismisses at 1.2s. ListSessions error arrives via existing SessionsMsg.Err path, no longer races loading-state. Phase 6 extends same seam for buffered stderr warnings.

**Do**:
- internal/tui/model.go:
  - Declare `type BootstrapCompleteMsg struct{}` adjacent to LoadingMinElapsedMsg.
  - In Init when `m.activePage == PageLoading`: batch `bootstrapCompleteCmd := func() tea.Msg { return BootstrapCompleteMsg{} }` alongside fetchSessions, loadProjects, loadingPadTick. Delivered on first event-loop iteration.
  - Update branch:
    - `case BootstrapCompleteMsg: m.bootstrapComplete = true; if m.activePage == PageLoading && m.minElapsed { m.transitionFromLoading() }; return m, nil`.
  - LoadingMinElapsedMsg branch (from task 5-6): make dismissal conditional on `m.bootstrapComplete`.
- internal/tui/model_test.go:
  - Tests: bootstrapComplete set but stays on PageLoading when minElapsed false; transitions when BootstrapCompleteMsg arrives after minElapsed; transitions when LoadingMinElapsedMsg arrives after BootstrapCompleteMsg; pads to 1.2s when fast; dismisses at bootstrap duration when slow; empty saved state still dismisses at 1.2s; orphaned BootstrapCompleteMsg post-transition harmless; Init does NOT emit BootstrapCompleteMsg when not on PageLoading.
- cmd/open.go:
  - No functional change. Add comment block above tea.NewProgram (line 391) documenting bootstrap-before-TUI ordering and dismissal-gate semantics: PersistentPreRunE ran orchestrator synchronously before this function reached. TUI's Init emits BootstrapCompleteMsg on first tick. Loading-page dismissal gated on (a) BootstrapCompleteMsg always delivered from Init AND (b) LoadingMinElapsedMsg after 1.2s via tea.Tick. Transition when both received.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- BootstrapCompleteMsg struct{} declared as public message type.
- Init when PageLoading: batches BootstrapCompleteMsg-producing command alongside fetchSessions/loadProjects/loadingPadTick.
- Init when not PageLoading: does NOT batch BootstrapCompleteMsg-producing command.
- Update BootstrapCompleteMsg sets m.bootstrapComplete = true.
- BootstrapCompleteMsg triggers transitionFromLoading iff minElapsed && PageLoading.
- LoadingMinElapsedMsg triggers transitionFromLoading iff bootstrapComplete && PageLoading (task 5-6 + this task agree).
- Fast bootstrap (<1.2s): page dismisses at exactly 1.2s.
- Slow bootstrap (>1.2s): page dismisses on BootstrapCompleteMsg receipt.
- Empty saved state still dismisses at 1.2s minimum.
- Orphaned BootstrapCompleteMsg post-transition is no-op.
- cmd/open.go openTUI body has comment documenting ordering + dismissal-gate.
- No executable code change in cmd/open.go beyond comment.

**Tests**:
- it sets bootstrapComplete on BootstrapCompleteMsg but stays on PageLoading when minElapsed is false
- it transitions off PageLoading when BootstrapCompleteMsg arrives after minElapsed
- it transitions off PageLoading when LoadingMinElapsedMsg arrives after BootstrapCompleteMsg
- it pads to 1.2s when bootstrap completes in under 1.2s
- it dismisses at bootstrap duration when bootstrap completes after 1.2s
- empty saved state still dismisses at 1.2s minimum
- an orphaned BootstrapCompleteMsg after transition is harmless
- Init does not emit BootstrapCompleteMsg when not on PageLoading

**Edge Cases**:
- Both messages same event-loop tick: Bubble Tea processes sequentially; whichever first flips its flag; second sees both true and transitions. Deterministic.
- Bootstrap error: PersistentPreRunE returned non-nil; cobra tears down before openTUI; TUI never launches; no loading page shown. No in-TUI handling needed. Phase 6 surfaces error via cobra's existing error-return.
- TUI started on PageSessions directly (serverStarted=false): Init returns non-PageLoading path; BootstrapCompleteMsg never emitted; loading machinery never runs.
- Empty saved state: Restore() no-ops, SessionsMsg{Sessions: []} arrives normally; loading still pads to 1.2s because BootstrapCompleteMsg fires from Init regardless. Spec-accurate: pad is about UI-feel.
- Very long bootstrap (5s restore of 50 sessions): no 6s hard cap. Page stays until both received.
- Phase 6 will extend: BootstrapCompleteMsg carries []bootstrap.Warning slice; handler emits to stderr AFTER loading dismisses. Type stays struct{} for v1.

**Context**: Spec 'Loading-Page Minimum Display (TUI Only)' — quoted in task 5-6. Spec 'Bootstrap Flow → Return-to-Caller Timing → TUI path': bootstrap runs; loading page shows for minimum 1.2s (padded if restoration faster, natural if slower); TUI appears with populated picker; user selects → attach flow. Spec 'Observability → TUI interaction': While loading page active, direct stderr writes would corrupt rendered UI. TUI path **buffers** bootstrap warnings in memory during loading window; emits to stderr AFTER loading dismissed (before picker renders, or immediately before exit on fatal). CLI writes stderr directly. Both paths log same content to portal.log. Phase 6 will extend BootstrapCompleteMsg with buffered warnings slice; for now, struct{}. Existing cmd/open.go:349-406 openTUI called after PersistentPreRunE completes confirms ordering.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Loading-Page Minimum Display (TUI Only), Bootstrap Flow → Return-to-Caller Timing → TUI path, Observability → TUI interaction.

title: Integration test: @portal-restoring suppresses captures during skeleton-restore window
description:
**Problem**: Whole spec architecture hinges on subtle invariant: between orchestrator step 3 (set @portal-restoring) and step 6 (clear), every structural event fired by restoration itself (session-created, window-linked, window-layout-changed, pane-focus-out) must be no-op on save side — notify touches save.requested, but daemon's tick sees @portal-restoring=1 at entry and skips entire cycle. If invariant regresses, daemon's first post-bootstrap tick captures mid-build state and overwrites pre-reboot sessions.json. Silent and catastrophic. No unit test catches it — ordering/coordination invariant across save daemon, hook pipeline, restore sequence. Integration is only validation surface.

**Solution**: Add integration test in `cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap_integration_test.go`. Spins up isolated `tmux -L <unique>` socket (Phase 3 task 3-13 pattern). Seeds sessions.json with multi-pane saved session. Invokes orchestrator end-to-end. Verifies: (a) at least one structural hook event fired during restore window (non-vacuous); (b) save.requested may have been touched but did not cause capture (saved_at unchanged mid-window); (c) in-flight capture started BEFORE marker may commit pre-restore snapshot (per spec In-Flight Capture Atomicity); (d) first post-clear tick captures complete final state. Kills socket in t.Cleanup on both pass and fail.

**Outcome**: Regression guard fires any time marker-ordering invariant breaks. Definitive home for assertions Phase 2's @portal-restoring-awareness composes with Phase 5's orchestrator-enforced ordering.

**Do**:
- Create `cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap_integration_test.go` gated with `//go:build integration`.
- Scaffolding:
  - t.TempDir() for state dir; PORTAL_STATE_DIR env override (Phase 2 task 2-1).
  - Build portal binary once (`go build -o <tempdir>/portal .`); reuse cmd/root_integration_test.go pattern if exists.
  - Unique socket: `socket := fmt.Sprintf("portal-test-%d-%d", os.Getpid(), time.Now().UnixNano())`.
  - t.Cleanup: `tmux -L <socket> kill-server` (ignore error).
- Seed sessions.json: single saved session 'work' with 2 windows, 2-3 panes per window, plausible layout strings. Use Phase 2 task 2-3's encoder; fixture constants at top.
- Invoke orchestrator: real tmux.Client + RealCommander with TMUX_SOCKET_NAME=<socket>; bootstrap.NewOrchestrator(client); instrument step 3-6 with hookEventRecorder. Probe hook on isolated socket: `set-hook -ga session-created 'run-shell "echo session-created >> <tempfile>"'` (Phase 1's -ga append; probe coexists with Portal's). Run orchestrator's Run(ctx); capture step elapsed times via stepTimer wrapper.
- Assertions:
  - **(a) Non-vacuous**: probe-file contains ≥1 structural event between step-3 and step-6. If zero, fail with 'vacuous: no structural events fired during @portal-restoring window'.
  - **(b) No mid-window capture**: read sessions.json.saved_at pre-orchestrator. After step 6, before next daemon tick (sleep 1100ms), saved_at == pre-run value.
  - **(c) In-flight commit permitted**: saved_at == pre-run OR < step-3 timestamp; if between step-3 and step-6, fail 'capture committed during restore window'.
  - **(d) First post-clear tick captures final**: after step 6 sleep 1500ms (≥1s ticker + slack); read sessions.json; saved_at > step-6-clear timestamp; sessions include restored work with all windows/panes; environment non-empty if seeded; each pane has scrollback_file.
- Use testing.Short() to skip on short runs (~3s test).
- Fallback: if Phase 2/3 logging not finalised at Phase 5 landing, probe file + tmux hook is documented minimal dependency.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- New integration test in cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap_integration_test.go with //go:build integration.
- Isolated `tmux -L <unique>` socket — never user's tmux.
- Socket killed in t.Cleanup on both pass and fail.
- Seeds multi-session multi-pane sessions.json via Phase 2 task 2-3 encoder.
- Invokes bootstrap.Orchestrator.Run(ctx) end-to-end with real Client + RealCommander on isolated socket.
- Verifies ≥1 structural hook event fires during @portal-restoring window (non-vacuous).
- Verifies sessions.json.saved_at NOT advanced by capture during window.
- Tolerates in-flight capture started pre-marker committing during window.
- Waits ≥1.5s after step-6 and verifies first post-clear tick captured complete final state.
- Test passes `go test -tags=integration ./cmd/bootstrap/...`.
- Test skipped in testing.Short() mode.

**Tests**:
- @portal-restoring suppresses captures across structural events fired during skeleton-restore
- at least one structural event fires during the @portal-restoring window (non-vacuous)
- save.requested present during the window does not trigger a daemon tick
- in-flight capture started pre-marker is permitted to commit its pre-restore snapshot
- first post-clear daemon tick captures the complete final state with all sessions + panes
- isolated tmux -L socket is killed in t.Cleanup on both pass and fail

**Edge Cases**:
- Integration tests timing-sensitive. 1100ms / 1500ms sleeps conservative against 1s ticker; if cadence changes, sleeps need updating.
- If Phase 3 task 3-13 ships isolated-socket scaffold, reuse it.
- TMUX_SOCKET_NAME env vs -L flag: both work; prefer -L for clarity.
- Probe hook leaks: kill-server in t.Cleanup tears down all hooks.
- Timing flakiness: slow CI runner might miss 'no capture during window'. Mitigate with larger fixture (multiple sessions, many windows) so skeleton-restore takes longer than 1s tick interval.
- Test depends on Phase 2 tasks 2-11 (marker-aware capture) + 2-12 (ticker trigger). Failure could indicate Phase 2 regression, not Phase 5; diagnostic output should distinguish.

**Context**: Spec 'Ordering Rationale': @portal-restoring set in step 3 BEFORE _portal-saver in step 4 because step 4 fires session-created which hook pipeline would dirty flag. With ordering: marker set → first daemon tick no-ops; restoration runs → more events fire → all no-op on notify side; marker cleared → next daemon tick captures complete post-restoration state. Spec 'In-Flight Capture Atomicity': Capture cycle is single synchronous Go function. Checks @portal-restoring at entry only. If bootstrap flips marker mid-execution, in-flight completes normally and may commit. Safe: capture started before flag was capturing pre-restore steady-state (valid snapshot); writes atomic; rename via sessions.json. Spec 'Restoration guard': Daemon's tick checks @portal-restoring at top of cycle. While set (skeleton-restore window), no capture runs regardless of dirty-flag. Phase 3 task 3-13 established isolated-socket integration-test pattern; this task inherits scaffolding.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Bootstrap Flow → Ordering Rationale, Save-Side Architecture → In-Flight Capture Atomicity, Triggers & Serialization → Properties.

title: Integration test: end-to-end reboot round-trip with structure layout zoom CWDs env hooks ANSI
description:
**Problem**: Phase 3 task 3-13 ships save→restore round-trip test. Phase 5 needs tighter end-to-end exercising **full Phase 5 path**: save daemon captures multi-session config, simulate reboot (kill-server → fresh server on same socket with same state dir), invoke orchestrator Run, let client-attached fire via real attach-session, verify every spec property round-trips: session names, window structure, pane count, layout string, zoom flag, per-pane CWD, per-session env, per-pane resume-hook firing, per-pane ANSI scrollback. Without this test, regression in any single phase could break round-trip in ways unit tests cannot catch.

**Solution**: Mirror Phase 3 task 3-13 pattern but gate on Phase 5's orchestrator and exercise hydration via real `tmux attach-session`. Register resume hook with deterministic side-effect (sentinel file) so post-attach verification asserts hook fired exactly once per skeleton-restored pane. Vary base-index / pane-base-index across save and restore (variant 1: 0,0; variant 2: 1,1) to catch index-drift code paths in tasks 3-3, 3-5, 4-1.

**Outcome**: Single integration test certifies entire feature end-to-end. Any phase regression that breaks round-trip surfaces here first.

**Do**:
- Create `cmd/bootstrap/reboot_roundtrip_test.go` with `//go:build integration`.
- Variants table-driven, each subtest:
  - V1: base-index=0, pane-base-index=0; 2 sessions, 2 windows each, 2-3 panes per window.
  - V2: base-index=1, pane-base-index=1; same topology. Exercises index-drift mapping.
- Per-variant scaffolding: isolated tmux -L socket; temp state dir via env; start tmux with base-index/pane-base-index per variant; build save-time config (2 sessions via new-session/new-window/split-window); deterministic per-pane content with ANSI (printf with \033[31m); register hooks via PORTAL_HOOKS_FILE env mapping each pane key to `echo HOOK:<session>:<window>.<pane> > <state-dir>/hook-fired-<key>.sentinel`; set per-session env (LANG, TEST_VAR); zoom one pane in one window; invoke bootstrap.Orchestrator.Run(ctx); verify sessions.json saved_at recent.
- Simulate reboot: kill-server; start fresh server same socket same base-index; invoke orchestrator Run again; verify list-sessions includes both saved + _portal-saver.
- Trigger client-attached: spawn tmux attach-session in goroutine with fresh PTY (creack/pty or subprocess); wait 50-100ms for client-attached → signal-hydrate → FIFO signals → helpers dump.
- Wait up to 3s for all hook-fired-*.sentinel files (one per skeleton pane with hook).
- Verify post-restore: structure (list-panes -a -F); layout (#{window_layout} verbatim); zoom (#{window_zoomed_flag}); CWDs (display-message #{pane_current_path}); env (show-environment includes saved); hook firing (sentinel exists with exactly one HOOK:<key> line — once not duplicated per attach+session-changed); ANSI scrollback (capture-pane -e -p includes original \033[31m / \033[0m); markers cleared (show-options -sv filtered for @portal-skeleton-* zero entries).
- Subtest A: client-attached fires first.
- Subtest B: client-session-changed fires when switching via `tmux switch-client -t <other-session>`; second session's helpers also unblock.
- t.Cleanup kills socket.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- New integration test at cmd/bootstrap/reboot_roundtrip_test.go with //go:build integration.
- ≥2 table variants differing in base-index/pane-base-index — verifies drift path non-vacuously.
- Isolated socket; never user sessions.
- Socket killed in t.Cleanup pass+fail.
- Save phase: multi-session/window/pane fixture with env, one zoomed, deterministic ANSI scrollback.
- Reboot via kill-server + fresh server on same socket.
- Orchestrator Run invoked for both warming and post-reboot calls.
- client-attached exercised via spawned tmux attach-session on fresh PTY.
- client-session-changed exercised via tmux switch-client.
- Structure, layout, zoom, CWDs, env, hooks, ANSI scrollback all verified post-restore.
- Hook fires exactly once per skeleton-restored pane.
- @portal-skeleton-* markers cleared post-attach.
- Byte-level (or SGR-sequence-level) scrollback comparison.
- Skipped in testing.Short().

**Tests**:
- end-to-end reboot round-trip preserves structure, layout, zoom, CWDs, environment (per variant)
- resume hooks fire exactly once per skeleton-restored pane
- ANSI scrollback round-trips with colour / attribute sequences preserved
- base-index and pane-base-index drift does not break hook lookup (hook-key uses saved position)
- client-attached triggers hydration for the attached session
- client-session-changed triggers hydration when switching to a second saved session
- @portal-skeleton markers cleared after helper dump + 100ms settle
- _portal-saver daemon is live and capturing post-reboot (first post-clear tick verified)
- save-time and restore-time SGR sequences match on a representative pane

**Edge Cases**:
- PTY spawning: creack/pty pattern; if unavailable, spawn tmux attach-session as subprocess with stdin/stdout redirected (setsid + controlling-terminal). If too fragile for CI, fall back to invoking `portal state signal-hydrate <session>` directly (hook body would do same) — pragmatic tradeoff but reduces coverage.
- Timing: hydration helper has 3s FIFO timeout; signal well inside. 1s wait between attach spawn and sentinel polling.
- Index drift V2/V3: exercise paths in Phase 3 tasks 3-3 (saved --file/--hook-key passed verbatim), 3-5 (re-query live paneKey), Phase 4 task 4-1 (hook lookup uses --hook-key verbatim). Bug surfaces as missing sentinel or wrong-pane firing.
- Zoom + layout ordering: regression that applies zoom before layout produces wrong geometry. Verify both on restored window.
- Hook firing exactly once: design fires at hydrate-helper time; signal-hydrate idempotent across client-attached + client-session-changed; helper only receives one FIFO byte; second signal hits unlinked FIFO — no-op. Verify by spawning BOTH attach events and asserting sentinel line count == 1.
- ANSI comparison: PTY line-wrapping at different terminal width could shift bytes. Run with fixed geometry. If still flaky, compare SET of SGR sequences rather than byte-exact.
- _portal-saver post-reboot: daemon seeds hash map from disk (task 2-9) so first post-clear tick does NOT rewrite every scrollback. Verify scrollback file mtimes NOT all bumped after first post-restore tick (indirect: if bumped, hydration hadn't cleared markers in time or hash-seed broken).

**Context**: Spec 'Attach Flow (After Bootstrap)': 1. Portal resolves target; 2. switch-client if inside tmux else exec attach-session -A; 3. tmux fires client-attached (bare-shell) or client-session-changed (inside-tmux switch); 4. Hook runs portal state signal-hydrate <session>; 5. list-panes enumerate; per pane with @portal-skeleton-<paneKey> write FIFO byte; 6. Per-pane helpers unblock, dump scrollback, sleep 100ms, unset own markers, exec hook-or-shell; 7. Daemon's next tick captures now-hydrated panes normally. Spec 'Firing Point: Inside the Helper's Exec Chain': hooks fire from helper's exec chain after dump + settle + marker-unset. Spec 'Layout Restoration → Per-Window Restoration Order': new-window → split-window × (N-1) → select-layout → select-pane → resize-pane -Z if zoomed. Spec 'Helper hook lookup under index drift': Helper invoked with --hook-key '<raw-session>:<saved-window>.<saved-pane>' from sessions.json. Uses flag (not live position) to look up. Preserves hooks across base-index/pane-base-index changes. Phase 3 task 3-13 established save→restore round-trip test shape; this task extends into full reboot-cycle.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Bootstrap Flow → Attach Flow (After Bootstrap), Resume Hook Firing → Firing Point: Inside the Helper's Exec Chain, Layout Restoration, Save Format & Schema → Helper hook lookup under index drift.

title: Integration test: portal attach NAME and portal open NAME resolve sessions.json-only names
description:
**Problem**: Spec Phase 5 acceptance: portal attach NAME and portal open continue to resolve names that only exist in sessions.json at bootstrap time (skeleton created before command's attach logic runs). Task 5-5 removed bootstrapWait that previously masked this requirement (by waiting for sessions to appear). This task adds integration test: pre-seed sessions.json with session foo, start with zero live sessions, invoke portal attach foo and portal open foo (inside-tmux + bare-shell), verify resolve succeeds because orchestrator step 5 skeleton-created session before RunE ran. Also verify steady-state reattach (saved already live) is single JSON read + list-sessions + diff, no structural rewrites.

**Solution**: Add cmd/reattach_integration_test.go (or extend task 5-9 file). Five test cases: (1) bare-shell portal attach NAME sessions.json-only; (2) bare-shell portal open NAME path-argument; (3) inside-tmux portal attach NAME via switch-client; (4) steady-state same NAME live at start — Restore skips, no rewrites verified by pane_id unchanged; (5) negative NAME in neither — existing 'No session found' error path unchanged.

**Outcome**: Regression guard catching any future regression that re-introduces pre-attach wait or accidentally short-circuits Restore() for cases that need it. Proves spec's name-resolution-post-bootstrap acceptance.

**Do**:
- Create cmd/reattach_integration_test.go with //go:build integration. Or extend task 5-9 file.
- Common scaffolding (reuse from 5-8/5-9): isolated tmux -L socket; temp state dir; portal binary built once; subprocess invocations with TMUX_SOCKET_NAME, HOME, PORTAL_STATE_DIR env threaded.
- Test 1 — bare-shell attach sessions.json-only:
  - Pre-seed sessions.json with foo (1 window 1 pane).
  - Pre-condition: list-sessions does NOT include foo.
  - portal attach foo. Either (a) inject execer via attachDeps and verify HasSession(foo)==true after PersistentPreRunE; or (b) use PORTAL_TEST_NOEXEC=1 toggle that prints 'would exec: tmux attach-session -t foo' instead of execing.
  - Assert RunE doesn't return 'No session found: foo'.
- Test 2 — bare-shell open NAME path-argument:
  - Pre-seed sessions.json + matching alias OR direct-path.
  - portal open <path>; verify resolver lands on restored session.
- Test 3 — inside-tmux attach via switch-client:
  - Pre-seed sessions.json with foo. Create live host-session.
  - portal attach foo with TMUX=<socket>,<pid>,<pane> env (simulating inside-tmux). InsideTmux→true → SwitchConnector → switch-client.
  - Verify foo live + client switched (display-message #{client_session}).
- Test 4 — steady-state zero rewrites:
  - Pre-seed sessions.json with foo. Create foo live BEFORE running portal.
  - Capture pre-run pane id (display-message #{pane_id}).
  - portal attach foo.
  - Capture post-run pane id; assert equal → Restore did NOT rewrite. Verify saved_at not advanced during restore window (matching task 5-8 property).
- Test 5 — negative:
  - Empty sessions.json + empty live. portal attach nonexistent.
  - Assert non-zero exit; stderr contains 'No session found: nonexistent' (cmd/attach.go:37 unchanged).
- t.Cleanup kills socket per case.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- New integration test at cmd/reattach_integration_test.go with //go:build integration (or extension of task 5-9).
- Five explicit cases: bare-shell attach, bare-shell open, inside-tmux attach, steady-state zero-rewrite, negative not-found.
- Isolated socket per test.
- t.Cleanup kills socket.
- Tests 1-3 verify Restore creates skeleton so HasSession(NAME) returns true before RunE.
- Test 4 verifies Restore is no-op for already-live: pane_id unchanged; saved_at unchanged in restore window.
- Test 5 verifies existing 'No session found' error path works.
- portal open NAME path-argument tested (not just portal attach).
- client-attached + client-session-changed both exercised (tests 1 + 3).
- Skipped in testing.Short().

**Tests**:
- portal attach NAME resolves a name present only in sessions.json (bare shell)
- portal open PATH resolves a session name present only in sessions.json
- portal attach NAME resolves a name present only in sessions.json (inside tmux switch-client)
- steady-state reattach with saved session already live performs zero structural rewrites
- portal attach NAME returns the existing not-found error for names in neither live nor saved state
- has-session returns true for every name in sessions.json post-bootstrap
- saved_at is not advanced during a steady-state reattach window

**Edge Cases**:
- Bare-shell attach via syscall.Exec: testing exec handoff requires (a) intercepting via execer interface injection (pattern in cmd/open.go:173-184 realExecer) or (b) subprocess + exit codes. (a) cleaner for attach; extend AttachConnector with injectable execer, default realExecer, tests supply recording one.
- Inside-tmux detection: portal checks os.Getenv('TMUX'). Set in subprocess; or inject tmux.InsideTmux via package-level var.
- portal open resolver: buildQueryResolver reaches alias store + zoxide. For test, seed aliases (PORTAL_ALIAS_FILE env override) or path-argument via direct-path branch (simpler).
- Steady-state: panes created by skeleton-restore have fresh pane_ids different from pre-existing live. If Restore incorrectly re-created, pane_id would differ. If correctly skipped, preserved. Assertion shape.
- Negative case exit code: cmd/attach.go:37 returns wrapped error; cobra surfaces non-zero. SilenceErrors: true means cobra does NOT print — but exit code still non-zero. Test asserts exit code + checks stderr via injected test seam rather than relying on cobra printing.
- saved_at not advanced: reinforces task 5-8 assertion. Duplicated because different trigger (normal command vs test probe).

**Context**: Phase 5 acceptance: portal attach NAME and portal open continue to resolve names that only exist in sessions.json at bootstrap time. Spec 'Return-to-Caller Timing → CLI path': For `portal attach NAME` where target was in sessions.json, skeleton restored before attach logic runs, so has-session -t NAME returns true. Spec 'Restoration Trigger': If live tmux session exists with that name → skip. User's current reality authoritative; Portal never clobbers live. Steady-state cost (all saved already live): ~20ms — one JSON read + list-sessions + diff → no-op. Invisible. Existing cmd/attach.go:36-44 HasSession guard + 'No session found: %s' error unchanged. cmd/open.go:107-115 qr.Resolve switch unchanged; HasSession/path resolution happens with live tmux reflecting skeleton state.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Bootstrap Flow → Return-to-Caller Timing → CLI path, Restore-Side Architecture → Restoration Trigger, Phase 5 acceptance criteria.

title: Phase 6: Observability, User Commands, and Documentation
Introduce structured portal.log writer with timestamp/level/component/message format and PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL filtering
description:
**Problem**: Phase 2 task 2-7 landed minimal `internal/state/logger.go` with OpenLogger + four level methods used by daemon. Other components spec enumerates — restore, hydrate, notify, hooks, bootstrap — also need to log; call sites scattered. Across all writers format must be byte-identical (`timestamp | level | component | message`), timestamp RFC 3339 UTC regardless of zone, level filtering uniform via PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL. Today's Phase 2 logger satisfies daemon only. This task hardens shared logger into stable API every component relies on; specifies level-filter precedence (DEBUG < INFO < WARN < ERROR) so invalid PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL doesn't silently swallow or over-emit.

**Solution**: Harden internal/state/logger.go: (a) typed Level enum (LevelDebug/Info/Warn/Error) + parseLevel(env) → maps four canonical strings case-insensitive, returns LevelWarn on empty/unknown, debug = emit all; (b) every Debug/Info/Warn/Error(component, format, args) writes exactly one line: `2026-04-23T10:30:00Z | WARN | daemon | message\n` — UTC RFC3339 timestamp (never local), level upper-cased, component verbatim, fmt.Sprintf message; (c) embedded `|` written verbatim (separator is `" | "` with spaces, raw `|` unambiguous); (d) reject empty component via const catalogue (ComponentDaemon, ComponentRestore etc.) — typo = compile error; (e) Logger safe across goroutines via internal sync.Mutex around writes.

**Outcome**: Every subsystem writes structured lines with identical format, level filtering, UTC timestamps. Invalid PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL=foo behaves as default. PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL=debug enables all. Component constants enforce tidy column.

**Do**:
- Edit internal/state/logger.go:
  - `type Level int` with const (LevelDebug, LevelInfo, LevelWarn, LevelError).
  - `var levelNames = [...]string{"DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR"}`.
  - `func parseLevel(s string) Level` — trim+lowercase; map four canonical; anything else → LevelWarn.
  - Logger holds: file *os.File, mu sync.Mutex, threshold Level (resolved at construction from PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL).
  - OpenLogger(path, rotate) preserves Phase 2 rotation-on-open (rename portal.log → portal.log.old when rotate && size ≥1MB, replacing existing); O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY mode 0600; resolves threshold via parseLevel.
  - emit(level, component, format, args): return if level < threshold; build line via fmt.Sprintf("%s | %s | %s | %s\n", time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), levelNames[level], component, fmt.Sprintf(format, args...)); mu.Lock+defer Unlock; file.Write swallow error.
  - Debug/Info/Warn/Error wrappers over emit.
  - Close() closes file.
- Add component constants: ComponentDaemon="daemon", ComponentRestore="restore", ComponentHydrate="hydrate", ComponentNotify="notify", ComponentHooks="hooks", ComponentBootstrap="bootstrap". All existing logger.Warn("daemon", ...) call sites migrate to logger.Warn(state.ComponentDaemon, ...) in task 6-2.
- Tests in internal/state/logger_test.go: format; UTC timestamp regardless of local zone (set time.Local FixedZone TEST 4h); embedded | preserved (split with limit 4); default WARN filters DEBUG+INFO; debug emits all; info emits INFO+; invalid value defaults WARN; case-insensitive; concurrent safe (16 goroutines × 100 lines = 1600 well-formed); component constants exist.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- internal/state/logger.go exports six component constants with lowercase string values.
- Logger has Debug/Info/Warn/Error with same (component, format, args) signature; writes `timestamp | level | component | message\n`.
- Timestamps RFC 3339 UTC ending in Z.
- Level filtering case-insensitive; default WARN; unknown defaults WARN.
- Logger safe for concurrent use (internal mutex).
- Write failures never panic / propagate.
- Existing Phase 2 daemon call sites work (API superset).
- Embedded | written verbatim.

**Tests**:
- it writes entries in timestamp | level | component | message format
- it uses UTC RFC3339 timestamps regardless of local time zone
- it preserves embedded | characters inside messages verbatim
- it filters DEBUG and INFO when PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL is unset (default WARN)
- it emits all four levels when PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL=debug
- it emits INFO, WARN, ERROR when PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL=info
- it defaults to WARN on an invalid PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL value
- it is case-insensitive for PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL values
- it is safe to call from multiple goroutines concurrently
- it exposes component constants for daemon/restore/hydrate/notify/hooks/bootstrap

**Edge Cases**:
- State dir missing on OpenLogger: callers expected to state.EnsureDir() first (task 2-1). Logger does not auto-create.
- Invalid PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL → default LevelWarn, no error, no stderr noise.
- Multibyte UTF-8 messages preserved verbatim (fmt.Sprintf handles UTF-8).
- Embedded | written verbatim; grep users split with limit 4 (`awk -F ' \\| ' '{...}'`).
- UTC timestamp regardless of local zone — stable cross-machine grep+ordering.
- Empty component prevented at compile-site via constants catalogue. No runtime validation; signature still string for free-form.
- Close() idempotent-ish (second call os.ErrClosed); callers defer once per opened logger.
- Rotation only on OpenLogger(path, true); task 6-3 adds mid-write for daemon.

**Context**: Spec 'Log File → Format': single line per entry: `timestamp | level | component | message`. Timestamp RFC 3339 UTC. Level DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR. Component short identifier (daemon/restore/hydrate/notify/hooks/bootstrap). Message free-form. Spec 'Log File → Log level': warnings and errors by default. PORTAL_LOG_LEVEL=debug env var enables verbose tracing. Spec 'What gets logged': Save failures, restoration warnings, hydrate timeouts, helper crashes, bootstrap events at DEBUG. Phase 2 task 2-7 landed OpenLogger(path, rotate) with Debug/Info/Warn/Error already present. This task hardens — component constants, typed levels, explicit threshold parsing, mutex-guarded writes, UTC guarantee. Public surface preserved; Phase 2 tests remain green.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Observability → Log File, Observability → What gets logged.

title: Non-daemon writers append via O_APPEND with no size check; retrofit components to new logger
description:
**Problem**: Spec 'Concurrent-writer discipline': Only daemon rotates. Every other Portal writer appends via O_APPEND (POSIX atomic for writes < PIPE_BUF — trivially satisfied by one-line entry). CLI/helper/subprocess do not check size, do not rotate. Today's logger (Phase 2 + task 6-1) supports OpenLogger(path, rotate); daemon passes true, everyone else false. But callers scattered across Phase 3 restore/hydrate, Phase 4 hooks/migrate-rename, Phase 5 bootstrap, ad-hoc log.Println / fmt.Fprintln(stderr). Without retrofit, scattered approach defeats single-log-file grep workflow portal state status (task 6-4) depends on.

**Solution**: Audit every log call site in internal/state/*, internal/hooks/*, cmd/state_*.go, cmd/bootstrap/*, internal/tmux/hooks_*.go. For each: replace log/stderr with *state.Logger via state.OpenLogger(state.PortalLog(dir), false); pick appropriate ComponentX constant; pick level (Debug for bootstrap traces, Warn for recoverable, Error for fatal). Helper openLoggerNoRotate() returns NopLogger on error (logging never crashes callers). Short-lived commands defer Close(); long-lived (daemon, orchestrator) reuse single Logger.

**Outcome**: Repo grep for log.Println/Printf/Fatalf or raw fmt.Fprint(stderr) returns zero in resurrection paths. Every line routes through *state.Logger to portal.log. portal state status sees 'Recent warnings: N' populated by every subsystem uniformly.

**Do**:
- Repo audit with Grep for log.(Println|Printf|Fatalf|Print) and fmt.Fprint(ln|f)?(os.Stderr (excluding intentional stderr one-liners owned by tasks 6-8/9/10).
- Add cmd/state_common.go with openNoRotateLogger() returning real *state.Logger or NopLogger sentinel on failure.
- Add state.NopLogger() *Logger — sentinel whose emit is no-op.
- Migrate per subsystem:
  - cmd/state_notify.go (task 2-2): ComponentNotify; Warn on failures only (hot path). No DEBUG.
  - cmd/state_hydrate.go (3-8/9/10): ComponentHydrate; Warn on timeout (with --hook-key), file missing, hook read failure; Debug on dump (length, duration).
  - cmd/state_signal_hydrate.go (3-11): ComponentHydrate (same — part of hydration pipeline); Warn on FIFO retry exhaust; Debug on enumeration.
  - internal/state/restore.go (3-6): ComponentRestore; Warn on corrupt sessions.json, select-layout fallback, per-session skip; Debug on per-session success.
  - cmd/state_migrate_rename.go (4-3): ComponentHooks; Warn on hooks.json write failure; Debug on zero-match no-op.
  - cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go (5-2): ComponentBootstrap; Debug on step entry; Warn on EnsureSaver failure (soft); Error on fatal (also surfaces stderr via task 6-8 — spec requires both).
  - Daemon (cmd/state_daemon.go): already logs; confirm uses ComponentDaemon throughout; migrate any remaining fmt.Print.
- Each migrated site defers logger.Close() at enclosing function. Long-lived close on shutdown.
- Tests per subsystem: inject fake *state.Logger or real one in t.TempDir; assert known failure path emits line containing component name.
- Verify O_APPEND: pre-populate sentinel line; run code path; sentinel still first line (no truncation).
- Verify no rotation: pre-populate 2MB content; run non-daemon path; assert portal.log.old does NOT exist; portal.log still ≥ 2MB.
- Integration test: `non-daemon writer does not rotate even when log exceeds 1MB`.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- internal/state/logger.go exports NopLogger() *Logger.
- cmd/state_common.go provides openNoRotateLogger() returning real or NopLogger sentinel.
- Every subsystem logs via *state.Logger; zero log.Println/Printf/Fatalf in resurrection code.
- Every non-daemon caller opens with rotate=false.
- Log created on first write mode 0600 (O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY).
- Non-daemon caller does NOT rotate even when ≥ 1MB (integration verifies).
- Parent dir missing tolerated by lazy-create via state.EnsureDir.
- Permission error does not crash caller — degrades to no-op.
- Each migrated site uses correct ComponentX.
- Short-lived commands defer logger.Close() at command root.

**Tests**:
- non-daemon writer does not rotate even when log exceeds 1MB
- notify logs WARN to ComponentNotify on file-create failure
- hydrate logs WARN to ComponentHydrate on FIFO timeout
- signal-hydrate logs WARN to ComponentHydrate on FIFO retry exhaustion
- restore logs WARN to ComponentRestore on corrupt sessions.json
- migrate-rename logs WARN to ComponentHooks on hooks.json write failure
- bootstrap orchestrator logs DEBUG to ComponentBootstrap on step entry
- bootstrap orchestrator logs WARN to ComponentBootstrap on soft EnsureSaver failure
- bootstrap orchestrator logs ERROR to ComponentBootstrap on fatal @portal-restoring failure
- logger degrades to no-op when portal.log cannot be opened (permission error)
- openNoRotateLogger returns working logger when state directory exists
- openNoRotateLogger returns NopLogger when EnsureDir fails
- append semantics preserved — pre-existing sentinel line survives subsequent writes
- zero residual log.Println/log.Printf/log.Fatalf calls in resurrection code (repo grep)

**Edge Cases**:
- Parent dir missing → openNoRotateLogger runs state.EnsureDir() first; creates state/ mode 0700.
- Log permission error → OpenLogger returns error; helper returns NopLogger; caller continues as if logging no-op. Spec: 'permission error surfaces but does not crash caller.'
- POSIX atomic append for writes < PIPE_BUF (4096 Linux, higher macOS). One-line entries trivially smaller.
- Non-daemon on already-10MB log: writes new line; does not rotate; grows until next daemon start performs rotation. Spec: 'If no daemon running and log grows past 1 MB, continues to grow until next daemon starts.'
- Concurrent non-daemon writers: each opens own fd with O_APPEND; POSIX atomic append per write syscall. Each line intact; interleaving between lines accepted and well-defined.
- Migration must preserve existing test expectations — assertions on stderr from migrated sites updated to assert on log file or empty stderr.
- Fatal bootstrap (task 6-8) still emits stderr AND log — dual destination via two independent sinks.

**Context**: Spec 'Concurrent-writer discipline': Multiple Portal processes can log concurrently. To avoid rotation races, only daemon rotates. Every other writer appends with O_APPEND (POSIX atomic). CLI / helper / subprocess do not check size, do not rotate. If no daemon running and log grows past 1 MB, continues to grow until next daemon starts, at which point startup performs rotation check and rotates if needed. Spec 'What gets logged': Save failures, restoration warnings, hydrate timeouts, helper crashes, bootstrap events at DEBUG. Spec 'Fatal Bootstrap Errors': every fatal also logs to portal.log at ERROR — task 6-8 handles stderr half; this task ensures log-write half. Task 6-1 introduced component constants and level filtering; this task drives adoption across every subsystem.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Observability → Log File, Log Rotation → Concurrent-writer discipline, What gets logged.

title: Daemon-only mid-write rotation when next write crosses >=1 MB
description:
**Problem**: Phase 2 task 2-7 rotates at OpenLogger(rotate=true) call time — startup checks size, rotates if ≥1MB, opens fresh. Handles rotation across daemon restarts but not during long-running lifetime. Daemon that starts at 500KB and logs for weeks never rotates — file grows unbounded until next restart (version bump, crash, cleanup). Spec's 2 MB total budget violated. Add mid-write rotation: before each write, if pending would push file to ≥1MB, rename portal.log → portal.log.old first, open fresh portal.log, then write pending line. Only daemon does this.

**Solution**: Logger.rotating bool flag set by OpenLogger(path, true). On each emit, if rotating true, stat current file before writing; if Stat.Size() + len(pending) >= 1MB, close current fd, rename → .old (replacing existing), open fresh fd O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY mode 0600, then write. Check-then-rotate-then-write under existing sync.Mutex; daemon is only rotating writer (no cross-process race; other writers skip per task 6-2). Rename failure → single stderr diagnostic + continue with current file. Inclusive threshold: write that would push to exactly 1MB triggers rotation; lands in fresh file (keeps portal.log < 1MB at rest).

**Outcome**: Daemon running for weeks never lets portal.log exceed 1MB. When write would cross boundary, portal.log renamed to .old (replacing existing), fresh portal.log opened, write lands there. Non-daemon writers continue appending without size checks; if daemon down and they push past 1MB, next daemon start rotates on OpenLogger(true) per Phase 2 task 2-7 startup check.

**Do**:
- Edit internal/state/logger.go:
  - Add Logger.rotating bool field set from OpenLogger rotate arg.
  - Add Logger.path string field (logger knows its file path for rename + reopen).
  - `const LogRotateThreshold = 1 * 1024 * 1024` (exported for task 6-4 + tests).
  - Modify emit:
    - if level < threshold return.
    - Build line.
    - mu.Lock + defer Unlock.
    - if rotating: maybeRotate(int64(len(line))).
    - file.Write swallow error.
  - Add maybeRotate(pending int64):
    1. st, err := file.Stat(); on err log stderr + return (keep writing current).
    2. If size + pending < LogRotateThreshold return.
    3. file.Close (best-effort log).
    4. oldPath := path + ".old".
    5. os.Remove(oldPath) — replace existing, ignore ENOENT.
    6. os.Rename(path, oldPath); on err fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "portal: log rotation failed: %v\n"); reopen same file; continue.
    7. f, err := os.OpenFile(path, O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY, 0o600); on err fall back to stderr; leave l.file = nil (emit no-ops on nil).
    8. l.file = f.
- Tests in internal/state/logger_test.go: pre-populate threshold-200 bytes, emit ~100 (no rotate), emit ~150 (push past, rotate); rotation triggers at exactly 1MB; existing .old replaced; rename failure logs stderr + continues; triggering write in fresh file; daemon restart between size-check and rename tolerated; non-daemon never reaches code path.
- cmd/state_daemon.go already calls OpenLogger(path, true). No daemon code change.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- emit calls maybeRotate only when rotating == true.
- maybeRotate uses LogRotateThreshold inclusive boundary (size + pending ≥).
- Rotation replaces existing portal.log.old via os.Remove before os.Rename.
- Rename failure → single stderr diagnostic + continue current file.
- Triggering write lands in fresh portal.log (rotate-before-write keeps log < 1MB at rest).
- Non-daemon callers never reach maybeRotate (rotating flag gates).
- Existing Phase 2 startup-rotation continues unchanged.
- Logger.path populated at construction.

**Tests**:
- daemon rotates mid-write when the next write would cross 1 MB
- daemon rotation triggers at exactly 1 MB (inclusive threshold)
- existing portal.log.old is replaced on rotation
- rotation-rename failure logs to stderr and continues with current file
- the triggering write lands in the fresh portal.log
- daemon restart between size check and rename is tolerated
- non-daemon caller never reaches mid-write rotation code path
- startup rotation from Phase 2 task 2-7 still works

**Edge Cases**:
- Inclusive threshold: write equals 1MB triggers.
- Existing .old replaced via os.Remove before rename. ENOENT ignored. Other failures error rename + stderr fires.
- Rotation-rename failure: stderr once, keep writing current; transient conditions self-heal on next-write check.
- Write spanning boundary: per spec next write would cross ≥1MB triggers; write lands fresh; previous content in .old; portal.log < 1MB at rest.
- Daemon restart between check+rename: next daemon's OpenLogger(path, true) re-checks at startup and rotates again if still ≥1MB. Idempotent.
- Non-daemon never reaches rotation: rotating bool short-circuits maybeRotate top. Load-bearing invariant against rotation-race spec warns about.
- OpenFile fails in step 7: l.file nil; subsequent emit no-ops. Caller never crashes. Diagnosable via stderr one-liner.

**Context**: Spec 'Log Rotation': Simple 2-file cap at 1 MB per file. On reaching 1 MB during write, rename portal.log → portal.log.old (replacing existing), start fresh portal.log. Total disk bounded ~2 MB. Portal rotates itself in-process; no external logrotate dependency. Spec 'Concurrent-writer discipline': only daemon rotates. Other writers append O_APPEND without size check or rotation. If no daemon running and log grows past 1MB, continues to grow until next daemon starts, at which point startup performs rotation check and rotates if needed. Phase 2 task 2-7 landed startup-time rotation; this task adds mid-write for long-running daemons. Both coexist: startup handles 'daemon down → others grew file'; mid-write handles 'daemon up for weeks'.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — section Observability → Log Rotation.

title: Implement portal state status data collection (StatusReport + CollectStatus)
description:
**Problem**: Spec lists `portal state status` as primary user-facing diagnostic. Task 1-1 scaffolded stub; nothing populates today. Users run when they suspect Portal isn't saving/restoring; command must surface enough data to diagnose: daemon up? last save recent? state disk usage? recent warnings? This task implements **data-collection layer** — pure function gathering all six fields (daemon liveness with PID + version, last save, sessions count, panes count, state size, recent warnings) into StatusReport struct. Task 6-5 renders + computes exit code. Splitting collection from rendering lets future --json reuse collection unchanged.

**Solution**: Create internal/state/status.go: `type StatusReport struct` and `func CollectStatus(dir string, now time.Time) (*StatusReport, error)`. Reads daemon.pid + daemon.version (Phase 2 task 2-4 ReadPIDFile/ReadVersionFile), runs IsProcessAlive(pid), reads sessions.json (Phase 2 task 2-3 decoder) for counts + saved_at, walks state/ via filepath.WalkDir for total disk, scans portal.log for WARN/ERROR within last hour (parameterised as now - 1h for deterministic tests). Missing files → zero / 'never' / 'not running' per spec, never errors. Function only errors on genuinely exceptional conditions.

**Outcome**: Healthy Portal: DaemonRunning=true, PID=12345, Version=v0.4.2, LastSaveAt=recent, Sessions=10, Panes=34, StateSize=19088896, RecentWarnings=0. Unhealthy: fields populated accordingly; caller (task 6-5) computes exit code + renders.

**Do**:
- Create internal/state/status.go:
  - `type StatusReport struct { DaemonRunning bool, DaemonPID int, DaemonVersion string, LastSaveAt time.Time, HasLastSave bool, SessionsCount int, PanesCount int, StateSize int64, RecentWarnings int, LastWarning string }`.
  - `func CollectStatus(dir string, now time.Time) (*StatusReport, error)`:
    - Read PIDFile; if ok set PID + IsProcessAlive (otherwise DaemonRunning=false, PID=0).
    - Read VersionFile; if ok set DaemonVersion.
    - ReadSessionsJSON (Phase 3 task 3-1); if ok+non-nil set HasLastSave=true, LastSaveAt, SessionsCount, sum PanesCount across all sessions/windows.
    - computeStateSize(dir): WalkDir summing regular file sizes; ENOENT → 0.
    - scanRecentWarnings(PortalLog(dir), now-1h): parse line via SplitN " | " 4; ts via time.Parse(RFC3339); skip if before cutoff; level == WARN || ERROR else skip; count + last.
    - Return *StatusReport.
- Tests in internal/state/status_test.go covering: missing daemon.pid; live PID; dead PID; permission-denied PID treated as not-running; missing sessions.json; populated SessionsCount + PanesCount; LastSaveAt matches saved_at; missing dir → StateSize=0; sum of file sizes; scrollback/ subdirectory included; missing portal.log → RecentWarnings=0; portal.log.old NEVER scanned; counts WARN+ERROR within window; ignores INFO/DEBUG; tolerates malformed entries; uses caller-supplied now; LastWarning holds last valid line.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- StatusReport struct with all listed fields.
- CollectStatus(dir, now) returns populated *StatusReport.
- Missing daemon.pid → DaemonRunning=false, PID=0, no error.
- Dead PID → DaemonRunning=false.
- signal(0) permission denied → not-running, no error bubbled.
- Missing sessions.json → HasLastSave=false, counts=0, no error.
- SessionsCount = len(idx.Sessions); PanesCount = sum across all windows.
- StateSize = byte sum of regular files under state dir (including scrollback/).
- Missing state dir → StateSize=0, no error.
- RecentWarnings counts WARN+ERROR in last 1h from now.
- portal.log.old NEVER scanned.
- Missing portal.log → RecentWarnings=0, no error.
- Malformed entries tolerated, skipped without error.
- Only WARN and ERROR counted.
- LastWarning holds last valid WARN/ERROR line.

**Tests**:
- CollectStatus returns DaemonRunning=false when daemon.pid is missing
- CollectStatus returns DaemonRunning=true for a live PID
- CollectStatus returns DaemonRunning=false for a dead PID
- CollectStatus treats daemon.pid permission denied as not-running
- CollectStatus returns HasLastSave=false when sessions.json is missing
- CollectStatus populates SessionsCount and PanesCount from sessions.json
- CollectStatus returns LastSaveAt matching sessions.json saved_at
- CollectStatus returns StateSize==0 when directory is missing
- CollectStatus returns StateSize as sum of file sizes in state dir
- CollectStatus includes scrollback/ subdirectory contents in StateSize
- CollectStatus returns RecentWarnings==0 when portal.log is missing
- CollectStatus does NOT scan portal.log.old
- CollectStatus counts WARN and ERROR entries within the 1-hour window
- CollectStatus ignores INFO and DEBUG entries
- CollectStatus tolerates malformed log entries
- CollectStatus uses the caller-supplied now for the 1-hour window
- CollectStatus LastWarning holds the last valid WARN/ERROR line

**Edge Cases**:
- Missing sessions.json → counts=0, HasLastSave=false, LastSaveAt zero — renderer shows 'never'.
- Missing/unparseable daemon.pid → DaemonRunning=false, PID=0.
- signal(0) EPERM (PID belongs to other user) → IsProcessAlive false. Not error path.
- Missing state dir → StateSize=0. WalkDir ENOENT on root caught and returns 0.
- Missing portal.log → 0 warnings, no error. Healthy not failure.
- portal.log.old never scanned — historical. Test fixture asserts.
- Malformed entries (< 4 fields, unparseable timestamp) tolerated, scanner continues.
- 1-hour window inclusive of cutoff: ts.Before(cutoff) filters strictly older.
- now caller-supplied so tests pin cutoff deterministically.
- 1MB log scan ~16k lines at 64 b/line; bufio.Scanner handles in ms.
- Nested dirs under scrollback/ via WalkDir recursion.
- Symlinks: WalkDir follows by default; rare; counted; documented.

**Context**: Spec 'CLI Surface → portal state status': Output example: Save daemon running (pid + version); Last save 12 seconds ago; Sessions captured 10; Panes captured 34; State size 18.2 MB; Recent warnings 0 (last: none). Data sources: Daemon liveness via has-session + process verify; Last save time via sessions.json.saved_at; counts from sessions.json; state size total disk usage under state/; recent warnings scan portal.log within last hour. Spec 'Scan window semantics': 'Recent warnings' and exit-code 'recent errors' use **same one-hour window**, measured from now back over entries in current portal.log only. portal.log.old NOT scanned (historical). If portal.log doesn't exist (first-ever, no warnings yet), both count and exit-code treatment are zero/healthy — missing log never itself a warning. Spec 'Lifecycle Summary': prefers PID+signal(0) over has-session/pane_current_command (definitive). Task 6-5 optionally adds has-session sanity check but PID+signal(0) authoritative.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections CLI Surface → portal state status, Observability → portal state status, Save-Side Architecture → Lifecycle Summary → Liveness verification.

title: Render portal state status output and compute exit code
description:
**Problem**: Task 6-4 provides data (StatusReport). This task wires cmd/state_status.go RunE to call CollectStatus, render documented output, compute exit code. Rendering simple but display-formatting concerns: '12 seconds ago' relative time, '18.2 MB on disk' binary-unit, '(last: none)' vs '(last: <line>)'. Exit code three non-zero conditions: daemon not running, last save > 5 min ago, any recent WARN/ERROR in log. Spec excludes --json from v1.

**Solution**: cmd/state_status.go RunE: (1) state.ResolveDir (no create — read-only); (2) state.CollectStatus(dir, time.Now()); (3) render six-line output via fmt.Fprintln on cmd.OutOrStdout(); (4) compute exit code: 0 if healthy; non-zero if \!DaemonRunning OR (HasLastSave && now-LastSaveAt > 5min) OR RecentWarnings>0. Return ErrStatusUnhealthy sentinel; cmd.SilenceErrors=true so sentinel doesn't print extra (output already on stdout). Render helpers: formatDuration ('just now' < 2s, 'N seconds ago' < 60s, 'N minutes ago' < 60m, 'N hours ago' < 24h, 'N days ago' ≥ 24h; saved_at zero → 'never'); formatBytes (binary 1024 math but MB/GB/TB labels per spec example 18.2 MB).

**Outcome**: Healthy: 'Portal state:\n  Save daemon: running (pid 12345, version v0.4.2)\n  Last save: 12 seconds ago\n  Sessions captured: 10\n  Panes captured: 34\n  State size: 18.2 MB on disk\n  Recent warnings: 0 (last: none)' exits 0. Unhealthy: 'not running' / '7 minutes ago' / 'Recent warnings: 3 (last: <line>)' exits 1.

**Do**:
- Edit cmd/state_status.go:
  - Replace stub RunE: ResolveDir + CollectStatus + renderStatus + isUnhealthy → ErrStatusUnhealthy.
  - Set stateStatusCmd.SilenceErrors = true; SilenceUsage = true.
  - ErrStatusUnhealthy = errors.New("") — empty so Cobra prints nothing.
  - renderStatus(w, r, now): 'Portal state:'; daemon running/not running line; 'Last save: ...' or 'never'; sessions count; panes count; state size on disk; recent warnings line.
  - renderVersion(v): return v if non-empty else 'unknown'.
  - isUnhealthy: \!DaemonRunning OR (HasLastSave && now-LastSaveAt > 5min) OR RecentWarnings > 0. \!HasLastSave (fresh install) does NOT trigger unhealthy.
  - formatDuration switch: <2s 'just now'; <60s 'N seconds ago'; <60m 'N minutes ago'; <24h 'N hours ago'; else 'N days ago'.
  - formatBytes: const unit=1024; <unit 'B'; loop div, exp; '%.1f %cB' with KMGTPE[exp].
- Tests in cmd/state_status_test.go: healthy six-line layout; not running; never last save; (last: none); (last: <line>); exit 0 healthy; exit non-zero daemon down; exit non-zero stale save; exit non-zero recent warnings; exit 0 fresh install; version unknown; formatDuration table-driven; formatBytes table-driven (500 B, 1.5 KB, 18.2 MB, 2.0 GB); SilenceErrors true; SilenceUsage true; no --json flag.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- RunE calls CollectStatus + renderStatus.
- Output line-for-line matches spec example when healthy.
- 'Save daemon: running (pid N, version V)' for live; 'not running' otherwise.
- 'Last save: X ago' when HasLastSave; 'never' otherwise.
- 'State size: X.Y MB on disk' (binary math; B/KB/MB/GB/TB labels).
- 'Recent warnings: 0 (last: none)' or 'N (last: <line>)'.
- Exit 0 healthy; non-zero on three conditions.
- Healthy with no sessions.json yet (fresh install) exits 0.
- SilenceErrors + SilenceUsage = true.
- --json NOT present (v1 scope).
- Version 'unknown' when empty.
- No emojis, colors, tables — plain text.

**Tests**:
- it renders the healthy-state six-line layout
- it renders 'not running' when daemon is absent
- it renders 'Last save: never' when sessions.json is missing
- it renders '(last: none)' when RecentWarnings == 0
- it renders '(last: <line>)' when RecentWarnings > 0
- it exits 0 when healthy
- it exits non-zero when daemon not running
- it exits non-zero when last save > 5 minutes ago
- it exits non-zero when RecentWarnings > 0
- it exits 0 when HasLastSave is false (fresh install)
- it renders version 'unknown' when DaemonVersion is empty
- formatDuration covers expected bucketing (just now / seconds / minutes / hours / days)
- formatBytes covers B / KB / MB / GB bucketing
- SilenceErrors and SilenceUsage are set
- --json flag is not registered in v1

**Edge Cases**:
- Daemon down → non-zero regardless of other fields.
- Last save > 5 min ago → non-zero even if daemon up.
- RecentWarnings > 0 → non-zero.
- \!HasLastSave + DaemonRunning + RecentWarnings=0 → healthy. Fresh install not failure.
- State size 0 B on fresh install renders '0 B on disk'.
- Empty daemon.version → 'version unknown'.
- Very old save (7 days) → '7 days ago' + non-zero.
- 1 day → '1 days ago' (no singular/plural — utilitarian CLI tone).
- Binary-math: 18.2 MB = 18.2 × 1024 × 1024 = 19,084,083 B. Spec example '18.2 MB' implies binary math with MB label.
- Non-TTY (piped): same format, no ANSI. Portal fmt.Fprintln does not colourise.
- Command exempt from bootstrap (task 5-1 'state' in skipTmuxCheck); CollectStatus reads disk only. No tmux server running is fine.
- time.Now() captured once in RunE, passed to both CollectStatus and renderStatus + isUnhealthy for consistent cutoff.

**Context**: Spec 'CLI Surface → portal state status': Output example six-line layout. Exit code 0 healthy; non-zero notable problem (daemon not running, last save older than 5 minutes, recent errors in log). Spec 'Scan window semantics': consistent 1-hour window for display and exit code; portal.log.old not scanned; missing log healthy/zero. Task 6-4 produces StatusReport; this task renders + decides exit code. --json explicitly excluded v1 per spec silence on structured output + broader YAGNI.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — section CLI Surface → portal state status.

title: Add daemon-kill action to portal state cleanup (SIGHUP-final-flush via kill-session -t _portal-saver)
description:
**Problem**: Phase 1 task 1-9 shipped portal state cleanup with only hook-removal. Spec defines three actions in specific order: (1) kill _portal-saver, (2) remove hook entries, (3) optionally --purge. Action (1) relies on _portal-saver existing — Phase 2 landed. This task adds action (1): invoke kill-session -t _portal-saver delivering SIGHUP via PTY close; daemon's handler performs final atomic flush (Phase 2 task 2-12) and exits. Ordering matters: daemon-kill BEFORE hook removal so daemon's final flush captures pre-cleanup state. Idempotent: 'no _portal-saver session' is not an error.

**Solution**: cmd/state_cleanup.go: prepend killSaver(client) before existing UnregisterPortalHooks. killSaver runs HasSession('_portal-saver') first; if false return nil (idempotent). Else KillSession('_portal-saver'). Tmux closes session's PTY → kernel delivers SIGHUP → daemon's handler flushes → exits → tmux auto-destroys session. Failure for any reason OTHER than 'session-absent' logged + contributes to non-zero exit but cleanup proceeds to hook-removal. Partial failures never short-circuit — spec: 'cleanup never aborts partway to leave mixed state'.

**Outcome**: portal state cleanup with _portal-saver running kills saver first (daemon flushes), then removes Portal hooks, then (task 6-7) optionally purges. Failures logged + non-zero exit; remaining steps run. Second back-to-back invocation: clean no-op (zero failures, exit 0).

**Do**:
- Edit cmd/state_cleanup.go:
  - Extend CleanupDeps with SaverKiller interface { HasSession(name) (bool, error); KillSession(name) error }. *tmux.Client already implements both.
  - Refactor RunE to ordered action list with per-action error accumulation:
    1. Open logger (defer close).
    2. killSaver(client, logger); on err errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("daemon kill: %w", err)).
    3. If client.ServerRunning(): UnregisterPortalHooks; on err errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("hook removal: %w", err)).
    4. (Step 3 --purge lands in task 6-7.)
    5. Return errors.Join(errs...).
  - killSaver(c SaverKiller, logger):
    1. exists, err := HasSession('_portal-saver'); on err log Warn + return wrapped.
    2. If \!exists return nil (idempotent).
    3. KillSession('_portal-saver'); on err containing 'can't find session' treat as success (race with auto-destroy); other errors log + return.
    4. Log Info ComponentDaemon: 'killed _portal-saver; daemon will flush final state on SIGHUP'.
- Set SilenceErrors = true; SilenceUsage = true so joined-error output not doubly-printed.
- Tests in cmd/state_cleanup_test.go: ordered-call recorder asserts HasSession + KillSession before UnregisterPortalHooks; idempotent when absent (HasSession false → zero KillSession); KillSession 'can't find session' tolerated; KillSession permission error contributes to joined error containing 'daemon kill'; UnregisterPortalHooks still attempted after KillSession failure; INFO log to ComponentDaemon; back-to-back clean no-op; existing Phase 1 task 1-9 acceptance preserved.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- killSaver(client, logger) runs BEFORE UnregisterPortalHooks in RunE.
- killSaver checks HasSession('_portal-saver') first and short-circuits if absent.
- killSaver treats 'can't find session' from KillSession as idempotent success.
- Other KillSession errors logged to portal.log at ComponentDaemon/WARN and contribute to joined error.
- RunE accumulates errors via errors.Join; no short-circuit between actions.
- Second back-to-back invocation: clean no-op.
- INFO log: 'killed _portal-saver; daemon will flush final state on SIGHUP'.
- @portal-restoring set during cleanup still causes daemon to skip final flush per Phase 2 contract (daemon's business; integration test 5-8 already covers).
- Phase 1 task 1-9 acceptance (hook removal alone when no daemon) continues to pass.
- SilenceErrors + SilenceUsage = true.

**Tests**:
- it kills _portal-saver before removing hooks
- it is idempotent when _portal-saver is absent
- it tolerates KillSession 'can't find session' as idempotent success
- it contributes to non-zero exit when KillSession fails for another reason
- it still attempts UnregisterPortalHooks even when KillSession fails
- it logs the kill to portal.log at INFO level with ComponentDaemon
- a second invocation back-to-back is a clean no-op
- @portal-restoring set during cleanup still allows the command to complete

**Edge Cases**:
- _portal-saver absent → HasSession false → killSaver nil → no error, no KillSession call.
- KillSession 'can't find session' (race with tmux auto-destroy): idempotent success via strings.Contains. Wording varies but substring stable across 3.0+.
- Permission error / server-down: logged + contributes to joined error. UnregisterPortalHooks still runs.
- Daemon's SIGHUP handler flushes BEFORE exit. By time hook removal runs, daemon persisted. If daemon crashed before handler fired, save file as-of previous tick — acceptable degradation.
- @portal-restoring set during cleanup (bootstrap mid-restore concurrent with cleanup): handler checks flag and skips flush. No corruption; just lose in-memory changes since last tick. Spec documents trade-off.
- Back-to-back: HasSession false → no kill; UnregisterPortalHooks observes no Portal entries → no removals; exit 0.
- Ordering load-bearing: reverse (hooks first then kill) would mean final flush on hook-less server; daemon captures via list-panes/show-environment/capture-pane (none hook-dependent), so ordering is defensive against subtle side-effects rather than strict correctness. Kept in spec-mandated order.

**Context**: Spec 'CLI Surface → portal state cleanup': Actions in order: 1. kill-session -t _portal-saver to terminate daemon (SIGHUP → final flush). Idempotent: absent session not error. 2. Remove Portal's set-hook -ga entries via index-based set-hook -gu. Already-absent not error. 3. Remove ~/.config/portal/state/ only with --purge. Spec 'Exit codes': 0 all completed (including idempotent no-ops); non-zero one or more failed (set-hook -gu error, kill-session non-'session-absent' reason, --purge rmdir failure). Partial failures still attempt subsequent actions — cleanup never aborts partway. Failures also logged to portal.log. Spec 'Lifecycle Summary': Creation via has-session + new-session; kill + recreate is canonical lifecycle op; kill-session is teardown primitive. Spec 'Signal Handling → Handler behavior': 1. If @portal-restoring set, skip final flush. 2. Otherwise flush via AtomicWrite and exit.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections CLI Surface → portal state cleanup, Save-Side Architecture → Signal Handling, Save-Side Architecture → Lifecycle Summary.

title: Add --purge flag to portal state cleanup for state-directory removal
description:
**Problem**: Phase 1 task 1-1 declared --purge as parseable boolean with no body; task 1-9 left as stub. Spec action (3) requires actually removing ~/.config/portal/state/ when --purge passed: all state files (sessions.json, save.requested, daemon.pid, daemon.version, portal.log, portal.log.old, scrollback files, FIFOs) and state directory itself. Order: --purge runs AFTER daemon kill (task 6-6) AND after hook removal — purging while daemon alive would race writes; staged after hooks for tidiness. Defensive: refuse to remove paths outside resolved state dir (symlink-as-state-dir attack would otherwise be catastrophic).

**Solution**: Add purgeStateDir(dir, logger) running os.RemoveAll(dir) only after verifying path resolves cleanly. Hook into RunE after hook-removal, conditional on --purge. Missing dir not an error (idempotent). Symlink defensive check via filepath.EvalSymlinks asserting resolved path matches expected.

**Outcome**: `portal state cleanup --purge` on fresh install with 20MB state: kills daemon, removes hooks, deletes entire state/, exits 0. Without --purge: state dir intact (reinstall picks up). On already-gone state dir: exit 0 (idempotent). With portal.log held open by daemon: task 6-6 killed daemon, FDs released by purge time.

**Do**:
- Edit cmd/state_cleanup.go:
  - Read --purge via cmd.Flags().GetBool('purge') at top of RunE.
  - After hook-removal step: `if purge { purgeStateDir(dir, logger); on err errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf('purge state dir: %w', err)) }`.
  - Implement purgeStateDir(dir, logger):
    1. info, err := os.Lstat(dir); ENOENT → return nil (idempotent).
    2. If info.Mode() & ModeSymlink \!= 0: return error 'refusing to purge symlinked state dir: %s'.
    3. resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(dir); if resolved \!= dir return error 'state dir %s resolves to %s — refusing'.
    4. os.RemoveAll(dir); on err logger.Error(ComponentDaemon, 'purge failed: %v') + return err.
    5. Log Info: 'purged state directory %s'.
- Task 6-6 set up ordering infrastructure (joined errors, logger); this task adds step 3.
- Tests in cmd/state_cleanup_test.go: --purge absent state dir untouched; --purge removes state dir; idempotent on missing dir; INFO log; refuses symlinked state dir; refuses when EvalSymlinks resolves elsewhere; ordered-call: kill → hooks → purge; --purge contributes non-zero on RemoveAll failure (chmod 0000 file inside); FIFOs and .bin files swept; --purge absent keeps state dir even with other steps run.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Reads --purge via cmd.Flags().GetBool('purge').
- --purge absent: state dir untouched after cleanup.
- --purge present: purgeStateDir runs AFTER killSaver + UnregisterPortalHooks.
- Missing dir → nil (idempotent).
- Refuses symlinked state dir (defensive).
- Refuses paths whose EvalSymlinks resolution differs from given path.
- os.RemoveAll failure: logged ERROR + contributes to joined exit error.
- FIFOs, .bin scrollback files swept.
- Single info-level log line on success.
- --purge documented in command Short/Long help.

**Tests**:
- it does not touch the state dir when --purge is absent
- it removes the state dir when --purge is passed
- it is idempotent when --purge is passed on a missing state dir
- it logs the purge to portal.log at INFO level
- it refuses to purge a symlinked state dir
- it refuses to purge when the state dir resolves through a symlink chain outside itself
- --purge runs after daemon kill and hook removal
- --purge contributes to non-zero exit when os.RemoveAll fails
- purge removes FIFOs and .bin files alongside regular files
- --purge absent keeps state dir intact even when the other cleanup steps ran

**Edge Cases**:
- State dir missing → Lstat ENOENT → nil. Idempotent.
- State dir is symlink (user redirected): refuse — log WARN + non-zero exit. User must manually clean.
- State dir contains symlink (e.g., portal.log → /var/log/something): RemoveAll unlinks symlink not target. Safe. EvalSymlinks only resolves outer path; nested handled by RemoveAll semantics.
- State dir canonical resolution differs (e.g., ~/.config/portal symlinks to ~/Library/Application Support/portal on macOS): EvalSymlinks returns canonical; if mismatch refuse. Catches macOS migration artifact (cmd/config.go performs one-time migration). After migration: resolved matches if user has new layout. Err on safe side — any mismatch refuses; users with custom setups override via env var.
- FIFO files: RemoveAll handles on Linux+macOS. No special case.
- portal.log held open by daemon: task 6-6 killed daemon; FD closed by purge time. Sub-ms race tolerated; if FD still open, dir entry unlinked but blocks stay until daemon closes; user doesn't care.
- Per-file failures during RemoveAll: returns first error; partial cleanup + error reported. User retries.
- --purge on Phase-1-era state dir (only projects.json/aliases/hooks.json exist; no state/ subdir): ResolveDir returns ~/.config/portal/state/ which doesn't exist; Lstat ENOENT; no-op. Does NOT delete ~/.config/portal/ itself.
- Post-v1 install with state/scrollback/: full recursive removal works.

**Context**: Spec 'CLI Surface → portal state cleanup': Actions in order: 1. kill-session; 2. Remove set-hook -ga entries; 3. Remove ~/.config/portal/state/ only when explicitly requested via --purge. Default leaves state intact so re-install picks up where left off. Spec 'Exit codes': non-zero on --purge specified but rmdir failed. Partial failures still attempt subsequent actions. Spec 'Storage Location': state under ~/.config/portal/state/ resolved via configFilePath. Resolved path is what --purge targets. Env-var overrides respected automatically via state.ResolveDir(). Spec 'What gets logged': Save failures, write errors, permission issues — --purge failures are 'write errors' category, log at ERROR.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections CLI Surface → portal state cleanup, Save Format & Schema → Storage Location.

title: Emit single-line stderr + non-zero exit for fatal bootstrap errors
description:
**Problem**: Spec enumerates four fatal bootstrap failure modes that must terminate Portal with single-line stderr message and non-zero exit: (1) tmux -V fail (version < 3.0 or absent — Phase 1 task 1-2 produces copy); (2) EnsureServer fail; (3) set-hook -ga calls fail en masse; (4) @portal-restoring set-option fail. Task 5-2 orchestrator produces errors; task 5-3 propagates via PersistentPreRunE. But today's propagation is plain Cobra: 'Error: ' prefix + usage block, violating spec's 'single line, no banners, no colors'. This task enforces single-line + non-zero-exit contract.

**Solution**: Introduce `bootstrap.FatalError` sentinel (in cmd/bootstrap/errors.go) wrapping cause and carrying user-facing single-line message. Orchestrator returns *FatalError for each fatal condition. PersistentPreRunE propagates as-is. Top-level Execute() handler intercepts via errors.As and emits UserMessage on single stderr line, then os.Exit(1). Every fatal also logs ERROR to portal.log at point of detection (task 6-2 wired logging; this task confirms log-write before stderr).

**Outcome**: tmux 2.9 → stderr 'Portal requires tmux ≥ 3.0 (found 2.9). Please upgrade.' + non-zero exit + portal.log ERROR entry. EnsureServer fail → 'Portal failed to start tmux server: <underlying>'. Mass hook fail → 'Portal failed to register tmux hooks: <joined>'. @portal-restoring set fail → 'Portal failed to set @portal-restoring marker: <underlying>'. All four one-liners, no banners, no colors, non-zero exit. TUI path: PersistentPreRunE returns before openTUI; no TUI launches.

**Do**:
- Create cmd/bootstrap/errors.go:
  - `type FatalError struct { UserMessage string; Cause error }`.
  - `Error() string` returns UserMessage; `Unwrap() error` returns Cause.
  - `func NewFatal(userMsg string, cause error) *FatalError`.
- Edit cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go (task 5-2 Run):
  - Step 1 EnsureServer: NewFatal('Portal failed to start tmux server: ' + err.Error(), err).
  - Step 2 mass hook fail (typed shape from RegisterPortalHooks): NewFatal('Portal failed to register tmux hooks: ' + err.Error(), err). Partial failure NOT fatal — log WARN per failure, continue.
  - Step 3 Restoring.Set: NewFatal('Portal failed to set @portal-restoring marker: ' + err.Error(), err).
  - Step 6 Restoring.Clear: NewFatal('Portal failed to clear @portal-restoring marker: ' + err.Error(), err).
  - Each wrap site logs ERROR to portal.log via logger.Error(state.ComponentBootstrap, '<single-line>: %v', err) BEFORE returning.
- Edit cmd/root.go / main.go:
  - Keep SilenceErrors = false (Cobra default) so usage errors print Cobra's own way.
  - In main.go Execute(): `if err := cmd.Execute(); err \!= nil { var fatal *bootstrap.FatalError; if errors.As(err, &fatal) { fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, fatal.UserMessage); os.Exit(1) }; ...; os.Exit(1) }`. errors.As intercepts FatalError first; all other errors fall through to Cobra's default path (preserves usage-error UX).
- For tmux -V: in cmd/root.go after CheckTmuxAvailable/CheckTmuxVersion, if err \!= nil: return bootstrap.NewFatal(err.Error(), err).
- Tests in cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap_test.go (extending task 5-2): EnsureServer fail returns FatalError with specified message; RegisterPortalHooks mass fail returns FatalError; Restoring.Set fail returns FatalError; Restoring.Clear fail returns FatalError; FatalError wraps Cause for Unwrap; EnsureSaver fail does NOT return FatalError (regression — uses SaverDownError per task 6-9).
- Tests in cmd/root_test.go / main_test.go: FatalError from PersistentPreRunE → stderr single-line + non-zero exit; Cobra usage error still prints usage; every fatal logs ERROR to portal.log; tmux 2.9 fatal exact single-line copy.
- Acceptance: four fatal copy strings exact:
  - tmux: 'Portal requires tmux ≥ 3.0 (found <V>). Please upgrade.' (Phase 1 task 1-2 produces verbatim).
  - EnsureServer: 'Portal failed to start tmux server: <underlying>'.
  - Mass hook: 'Portal failed to register tmux hooks: <underlying>'.
  - @portal-restoring set: 'Portal failed to set @portal-restoring marker: <underlying>'.
  - @portal-restoring clear: 'Portal failed to clear @portal-restoring marker: <underlying>'.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- cmd/bootstrap/errors.go defines FatalError with UserMessage + Cause + Error + Unwrap.
- Orchestrator returns *FatalError for steps 1, 2 (mass), 3, 6.
- EnsureSaver does NOT return FatalError — uses SaverDownError (task 6-9).
- Every fatal wrap site logs ERROR to portal.log with ComponentBootstrap BEFORE return.
- main.go/Execute() intercepts *FatalError via errors.As, emits UserMessage on single stderr line, os.Exit(1).
