- Stderr exactly one line (no banners, colors, usage block).
- Cobra usage errors still print Cobra default output.
- Four fatal user-message copies match spec verbatim.
- TUI path: openTUI never called on fatal; no loading page to tear down.
- Exit code distinguishable from Cobra usage errors via absence of usage-block stderr signal.

**Tests**:
- EnsureServer failure returns a FatalError with the specified user message
- RegisterPortalHooks mass failure returns a FatalError
- Restoring.Set failure returns a FatalError
- Restoring.Clear failure returns a FatalError
- EnsureSaver failure does NOT return a FatalError (soft path)
- FatalError wraps the underlying cause via Unwrap
- Execute() emits FatalError.UserMessage on a single stderr line and exits non-zero
- Cobra usage error still prints usage block (unknown command)
- tmux 2.9 fatal produces exact single-line copy
- every fatal also logs ERROR to portal.log with ComponentBootstrap
- stderr output contains no banners, colors, or ANSI codes on fatal

**Edge Cases**:
- tmux -V fail: Phase 1 task 1-2 surfaces from CheckTmuxVersion; PersistentPreRunE wraps in FatalError if not already one.
- EnsureServer fail: permission error starting tmux, socket-dir unwritable etc. Wrapped at step 1.
- Hook reg: partial failure (some events OK, some failed) NOT fatal — log WARN per failed event continue. Mass failure (all-or-none typed shape) IS fatal. Implementation: RegisterPortalHooks returns typed error indicating 'all events failed'; orchestrator promotes only that to FatalError.
- @portal-restoring set fail: rare (only if tmux died between steps 2-3). Fatal because daemon's restore-guard depends on marker.
- @portal-restoring clear fail: rare but critical; leaving marker set means daemon ticks skip forever, saves pause forever. Fatal.
- EnsureSaver soft (task 6-9) — user can use Portal without save daemon; sessions just don't capture.
- Multiple fatals in same invocation: impossible by construction — orchestrator short-circuits on first.
- errors.As correctness: FatalError pointer receiver; errors.As(err, &fatal) where fatal is *bootstrap.FatalError matches.
- Stderr unbuffered (line-buffered by default; Fprintln flushes on newline).
- Exit code: os.Exit(1) for all fatals. Cobra usage exits via own path (also 1). Distinguishability via stderr content not exit code itself. Spec 'distinguishable from Cobra usage errors' achieved via single-line-no-usage-block signal.

**Context**: Spec 'Fatal Bootstrap Errors': Soft failures degrade locally and continue. Fatal — underlying machinery can't even start — handled differently: tmux -V check fails → emit user-facing error immediately to stderr, exit non-zero, does not enter TUI. EnsureServer fails → emit stderr, exit non-zero. set-hook -ga calls fail en masse → log, emit one-line stderr; on TUI path dismiss loading page cleanly, emit error, exit non-zero. @portal-restoring set-option fails → same as set-hook failure. TUI path: loading page never hangs forever; unrecoverable error tears down Bubble Tea cleanly, emits error, exits. Spec 'Proactive Health Signals → Exception': Portal emits single line to stderr when critical problem detected at bootstrap. One line. No banners, no colors, no interactive UI. Spec 'Runtime version check': errors out with clear user-facing message if version below 3.0 (e.g., 'Portal requires tmux ≥ 3.0 (found 2.9). Please upgrade.'). Phase 5 task 5-2 Run returns errors at each step; this task types them as *FatalError where spec mandates fatal. Task 5-3 PersistentPreRunE propagates; this task ensures path preserves typed sentinel.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Observability → Fatal Bootstrap Errors, Proactive Health Signals, Scope & Constraints → Minimum Versions → Runtime version check.

title: Emit single-line stderr warnings for soft bootstrap failures (CLI path direct write)
description:
**Problem**: Spec defines two soft bootstrap warnings as single-line stderr one-liners without terminating Portal: (1) Corrupt sessions.json — restoration skipped, bootstrap continues, user sees empty state. Copy: 'Portal state file is corrupt — restoration skipped.' + 'Check \`portal state status\` or ~/.config/portal/state/portal.log.' (2) _portal-saver failed to start after retries — daemon didn't come up, no captures, bootstrap continues. Copy: 'Portal save daemon failed to start — sessions won't be captured.' + 'Run \`portal state status\` for details.' Today's orchestrator (task 5-2) treats these as soft. CLI path needs to write warning lines to stderr directly; TUI path (task 6-10) buffers and emits after loading dismisses. Both also log WARN to portal.log via task 6-2.

**Solution**: Introduce `bootstrap.Warning` sentinel carrying single-line stderr message + follow-up lines. Orchestrator collects warnings during Run into []Warning slice returned alongside error. PersistentPreRunE inspects slice: CLI path writes each warning's lines to stderr immediately. TUI path buffers in shared sink for emission post-dismissal. Shared cmd.BootstrapWarningsSink (in-memory slice + mutex) is canonical carrier.

**Outcome**: User with corrupt sessions.json runs portal list: stderr 'Portal state file is corrupt — restoration skipped.\nCheck \`portal state status\` or ~/.config/portal/state/portal.log.' then list outputs empty session list, exits 0. Daemon-down case: 'Portal save daemon failed to start — sessions won't be captured.\nRun \`portal state status\` for details.' Both also in portal.log at WARN.

**Do**:
- Extend cmd/bootstrap/errors.go:
  - `type Warning struct { Lines []string }`.
  - `func CorruptSessionsJSONWarning() Warning` returns spec-exact two-line copy.
  - `func SaverDownWarning() Warning` returns spec-exact two-line copy.
- Extend cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go Orchestrator:
  - Add `Warnings []Warning` field accumulated during Run.
  - Step 4 (EnsureSaver) on persistent failure: append SaverDownWarning. Also log WARN ComponentBootstrap.
  - Step 5 (Restore) on `errors.Is(err, restore.ErrCorruptIndex)`: append CorruptSessionsJSONWarning.
  - Change Run signature to (bool, []Warning, error).
- Create cmd/bootstrap_warnings.go:
  - `type BootstrapWarningsSink struct { mu sync.Mutex; warnings []bootstrap.Warning }` with Add, Drain, EmitTo methods.
  - Package-level `var bootstrapWarnings = &BootstrapWarningsSink{}`.
- Edit cmd/root.go PersistentPreRunE (task 5-3):
  - After orchestrator.Run returns warnings: `for _, w := range warnings { bootstrapWarnings.Add(w) }`.
  - Detect TUI path: if cmd.Name() == 'open' && len(args) == 0, defer to openTUI (task 6-10). Otherwise emit via bootstrapWarnings.EmitTo(cmd.ErrOrStderr()) BEFORE returning.
  - Cleanest: openTUI receives sink via context or openDeps; drains AFTER loading dismisses (task 6-10). PersistentPreRunE emits for every other command.
- Tests in cmd/root_test.go / cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap_test.go: corrupt sessions.json produces CorruptSessionsJSONWarning with exact spec copy; EnsureSaver persistent failure produces SaverDownWarning with exact spec copy; orchestrator Warnings accumulates multiple; CLI path emits to stderr; multiple warnings each on own lines in order; portal open with no args (TUI) does NOT emit (sink retains for openTUI to drain); both also log WARN to portal.log; sink Drain returns + clears atomically; sink safe for concurrent Add+Drain.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Warning struct with Lines []string.
- CorruptSessionsJSONWarning() returns exact spec two-line copy.
- SaverDownWarning() returns exact spec two-line copy.
- Run signature (bool, []Warning, error).
- Orchestrator accumulates SaverDownWarning in step 4 on persistent EnsureSaver failure.
- Orchestrator accumulates CorruptSessionsJSONWarning in step 5 on errors.Is(err, restore.ErrCorruptIndex).
- Each warning also logs WARN to portal.log with ComponentBootstrap at detection (task 6-2 logger reused).
- BootstrapWarningsSink with Add/Drain/EmitTo; package-level bootstrapWarnings.
- PersistentPreRunE emits sink to stderr for non-TUI commands BEFORE returning.
- Does NOT drain when portal open + zero args (TUI path); task 6-10 drains.
- Each warning's lines emitted in order, one per line, no banners/prefixes.
- Multiple warnings produce interleaved output (all first's lines then all second's etc.).

**Tests**:
- corrupt sessions.json produces CorruptSessionsJSONWarning with exact spec copy
- EnsureSaver persistent failure produces SaverDownWarning with exact spec copy
- orchestrator's Warnings slice accumulates multiple soft failures
- PersistentPreRunE emits warnings to stderr on the CLI path
- PersistentPreRunE emits multiple warnings each on their own lines in order
- PersistentPreRunE does NOT emit warnings when the command is portal open with no args (TUI path)
- both warnings also log WARN to portal.log with ComponentBootstrap
- sink's Drain returns warnings and clears the buffer atomically
- BootstrapWarningsSink is safe for concurrent Add and Drain

**Edge Cases**:
- Zero warnings: Drain returns empty; EmitTo no-op; no stderr noise.
- Multiple warnings (corrupt sessions.json + daemon down): both appear, each on own line, in orchestrator-observation order.
- CLI vs TUI detection: PersistentPreRunE inspects cmd.Name() and args. When cmd.Name() == 'open' && len(args) == 0, defer emission to openTUI (task 6-10). Else emit immediately.
- EnsureSaver soft (warns + continues); EnsureServer fatal (terminates via task 6-8). Orchestrator disambiguates by step.
- CorruptSessionsJSONWarning emitted for ANY parse error in sessions.json. Phase 3 task 3-1 reader defines error type.
- Log-side write (task 6-2) fires at detection point inside Restore/EnsureSaver, BEFORE orchestrator appends to Warnings. Single WARN preferred; document orchestrator appends AFTER detection-site logger.Warn so by stderr emission time portal.log already has matching line.
- Soft warning during FatalError-inducing bootstrap: orchestrator short-circuits on fatal, no soft warnings emitted. Fatal stderr is sole surface.
- Buffer not persisted across Portal invocations — per-process state. Each `portal <cmd>` gets fresh bootstrap + fresh sink.

**Context**: Spec 'Proactive Health Signals → Exception': Exception: genuinely broken states detected during PersistentPreRunE. Portal emits single line to stderr when critical problem detected at bootstrap. Two examples: _portal-saver cannot be created after retries (two-line copy); sessions.json exists but is unparseable (two-line copy). One line. No banners, no colors, no interactive UI. Spec 'TUI interaction': While Bubble Tea loading page active, direct stderr writes would corrupt rendered UI. TUI path therefore **buffers** bootstrap warnings in memory during loading window and emits to stderr AFTER loading dismissed (before picker renders, or immediately before exit on fatal). CLI path writes stderr directly. Both log same content to portal.log regardless of stderr behaviour. Spec 'Failure Modes': sessions.json corrupt → log warning, emit one-line stderr warning, skip restoration, continue bootstrap. _portal-saver creation fails → retry small number; persistent failure log, emit stderr warning, continue bootstrap without save daemon. 'One line warning' means PRIMARY message is one line; second line is follow-up pointer. Implementation emits both lines verbatim per spec example.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Observability → Proactive Health Signals, Failure Modes & Recovery → Consolidated Failure-Handling Table.

title: TUI buffers bootstrap warnings during loading window and flushes after page dismissal
description:
**Problem**: Task 6-9 handles CLI path — stderr emission immediate in PersistentPreRunE. TUI path requires different treatment: while Bubble Tea loading page rendering, direct stderr writes corrupt UI (alt-screen mode intermingles with error text). TUI path must buffer warnings and emit AFTER loading page dismissed (before picker renders), or immediately before fatal-induced exit. Fatal errors after TUI launches (rare) and panics during loading: TUI program torn down cleanly before stderr write so terminal is sane.

**Solution**: TUI already has seam: task 5-7's BootstrapCompleteMsg signals orchestrator finished. Extend BootstrapCompleteMsg to carry `Warnings []bootstrap.Warning` (repurposing empty struct — task 5-7 edge-case flagged this). Update handler stashes warnings on model; transitionFromLoading queues stderr emission via `tea.Sequence(ExitAltScreen, emit, EnterAltScreen)` — atomic sequence leaving alt-screen, writing stderr, re-entering. Matches spec 'before TUI picker renders'. Picker renders on re-enter; warnings on user's scrollback just before. Sink Drain idempotent (returns empty on second call), so dual emission safe. Fatal during loading: PersistentPreRunE handles before openTUI reached (task 5-2 synchronous, task 5-3 bootstrapOnce.Do completes before RunE called) — TUI never launches on fatal.

**Outcome**: User with corrupt sessions.json runs portal open: TUI loading page shows 1.2s, dismisses cleanly (alt-screen exited), stderr shows two-line warning, then picker renders (alt-screen re-entered). User proceeds normally. No warnings → no alt-screen toggle, no stderr — seamless.

**Do**:
- Edit internal/tui/model.go (extends task 5-7):
  - Change `BootstrapCompleteMsg` from struct{} to `struct { Warnings []bootstrap.Warning }`.
  - Add Model.bufferedWarnings []bootstrap.Warning.
  - Update BootstrapCompleteMsg branch: m.bootstrapComplete = true; m.bufferedWarnings = msg.Warnings; if PageLoading && minElapsed → return m, m.transitionFromLoadingCmd().
  - Update LoadingMinElapsedMsg branch: m.minElapsed = true; if PageLoading && bootstrapComplete → return m, m.transitionFromLoadingCmd().
  - transitionFromLoadingCmd():
    1. m.activePage = PageSessions; sessionsLoaded = true; evaluateDefaultPage().
    2. If len(m.bufferedWarnings) == 0 return nil.
    3. warnings := m.bufferedWarnings; m.bufferedWarnings = nil.
    4. Return tea.Sequence(tea.ExitAltScreen, func() tea.Msg { for w in warnings; for line in w.Lines: fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, line); return nil }, tea.EnterAltScreen).
- Edit cmd/open.go openTUI:
  - Drain bootstrapWarnings (task 6-9 sink) at openTUI entry.
  - Pass to model via Model.SetPendingBootstrapWarnings(warnings) — new setter.
  - Init emits BootstrapCompleteMsg{Warnings: m.pendingBootstrapWarnings}.
- Tests in internal/tui/model_test.go: buffers Warnings into Model.bufferedWarnings; transitionFromLoadingCmd returns nil when empty (no toggle no emit); returns tea.Sequence when non-empty; emits all lines in order; does NOT emit twice on repeat transitions; fatal during loading does not reach this path (regression).
- Tests in cmd/open_test.go: openTUI drains and passes to TUI model; openTUI drains empty when no warnings; openTUI emits via TUI path not stderr direct.
- Integration test (subprocess + tea.WithInput/WithOutput): TUI flushes buffered to stderr after loading dismisses.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- BootstrapCompleteMsg carries Warnings []bootstrap.Warning (non-empty struct).
- Model.bufferedWarnings stores warnings between message and transition.
- transitionFromLoadingCmd returns nil when bufferedWarnings empty (zero-noise case).
- Returns tea.Sequence(ExitAltScreen, emit, EnterAltScreen) when warnings present.
- Warnings emitted in order (outer slice + inner Lines preserved).
- NOT emitted twice across repeat transitions — bufferedWarnings cleared.
- openTUI drains global bootstrapWarnings sink at entry; passes to model via SetPendingBootstrapWarnings.
- Init emits BootstrapCompleteMsg{Warnings: pendingBootstrapWarnings}.
- CLI path (task 6-9) and TUI path (this) use same sink; never emit twice across paths.
- Log-side write (task 6-2) fires regardless of path; portal.log has warnings regardless of stderr buffering.
- Fatal handled by task 6-8 BEFORE TUI launches — no in-TUI fatal-handling code in this task.

**Tests**:
- it buffers Warnings from BootstrapCompleteMsg into Model.bufferedWarnings
- transitionFromLoadingCmd returns nil when bufferedWarnings is empty
- transitionFromLoadingCmd returns a tea.Sequence when bufferedWarnings is non-empty
- it emits all warning lines in order on transition
- it does NOT emit warnings twice on repeat transitions
- openTUI drains bootstrapWarnings and passes them to the TUI model
- openTUI drains returns empty slice when no warnings were accumulated
- TUI flushes buffered warnings to stderr after loading page dismisses
- empty warnings slice does not toggle alt-screen
- log file always receives warnings regardless of stderr buffering path

**Edge Cases**:
- Zero warnings → no alt-screen toggle, no stderr — seamless. Test asserts (regression vs spurious flickers).
- Multiple warnings → all emitted in order interleaved within single tea.Sequence.
- Fatal during loading: doesn't happen — task 6-8 handles in PersistentPreRunE before openTUI. TUI never launches on fatal.
- TUI panic during loading: Bubble Tea built-in recovery handles teardown; Portal doesn't intervene.
- Per-model-instance buffer; rare TUI re-launch within same process — sink Drain atomic returns current + clears; subsequent Drain empty. Safe.
- Sink package-level in cmd/; both CLI and TUI paths drain from same place.
- Log ALWAYS receives warnings — task 6-2 logs at detection, before either path emits. No stderr path affects log.
- tea.Sequence requires Bubble Tea v0.24+; verify version. Fallback: manual sequence Cmd doing ExitAltScreen + Print + EnterAltScreen in one function.
- Re-entering alt-screen after stderr write: brief flash showing regular terminal (where warnings appear), then re-enters for picker. Acceptable — spec says 'before picker renders' which this satisfies.

**Context**: Spec 'Proactive Health Signals → TUI interaction': While Bubble Tea loading page active, direct stderr writes corrupt rendered UI. TUI path **buffers** bootstrap warnings in memory during loading window and emits to stderr AFTER loading dismissed (before picker renders, or immediately before exit on fatal). CLI writes stderr directly. Both log same content to portal.log regardless of stderr behaviour. Spec 'Fatal Bootstrap Errors → TUI path': loading page never hangs forever; unrecoverable error tears down Bubble Tea cleanly, emits error, exits. Loading kept up only while bootstrap making progress. Phase 5 task 5-7 Edge Cases noted: Phase 6 extends seam — BootstrapCompleteMsg carries []bootstrap.Warning; handler emits to stderr AFTER loading dismisses. Type stays struct{} for v1. This task fulfils that phase-5-anticipated extension.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — sections Observability → Proactive Health Signals → TUI interaction, Observability → Fatal Bootstrap Errors.

title: Ship README updates: Privacy Uninstall hooks-on-reboot tmux>=3.0 storage location
description:
**Problem**: Feature ships user-visible behavioural change (scrollback persisted to disk, hooks fire only on reboot recovery, tmux ≥ 3.0 required, ~/.config/portal/state/ new directory). Without docs, users discover via surprise. Spec 'Documentation Deliverables' lists five concrete README additions: Privacy Considerations, Uninstall, hooks behaviour clarification, tmux version requirement, storage location. This task lands all five as distinct README edits, adhering to spec non-goals (no exhaustive tmux API, no architecture diagrams, no changelog).

**Solution**: Edit README.md in-place with five distinct additions: (1) Privacy Considerations section (after Configuration before License) — covers 0600 file mode, local-filesystem trust model, no encryption at rest, tmux history-limit 0 / clear-history workarounds; (2) Uninstall section (near end) — both paths: just remove binary, or portal state cleanup first then remove. Optional --purge for full state wipe; (3) Hooks clarification (edit existing xctl hooks section) — note hooks fire on reboot recovery not every detach/reattach; (4) tmux ≥ 3.0 requirement (edit Install) — Requirements subsection; (5) Storage location (edit Configuration table) — extend file table to include state/ directory with PORTAL_STATE_DIR env override.

**Outcome**: User reading README pre-install understands: scrollback persisted with what permissions and how to opt out; how to uninstall cleanly (both paths); hooks fire on reboot recovery only (behaviour change); tmux 3.0 hard requirement with upgrade path; ~/.config/portal/state/ joins existing config files. Pre-feature Portal user upgrading reads README to understand why behaviour changed.

**Do**:
- Edit README.md with five distinct additions:
  - **## Privacy Considerations** (before ## License): scrollback persisted to ~/.config/portal/state/, mode 0600, dirs 0700; matches shell-history trust model; no encryption at rest; mitigations: `tmux set-option -w history-limit 0` prevents accumulation, `tmux clear-history` clears on demand (run before next save at most 30s later); v1 has no per-session opt-out, tmux-native workarounds are supported path.
  - **## Uninstall** section: Two paths. (1) Just remove binary — `brew uninstall portal` or `rm $(which portal)`. Defensive `command -v portal` hook short-circuits when binary absent; data preserved; reinstall picks up where left off. (2) Explicit teardown — `portal state cleanup` (kills daemon + removes hooks); `portal state cleanup --purge` (also wipes state); then uninstall binary. --purge for completely clean slate; non-state config (hooks.json/projects.json/aliases) kept; remove manually if desired.
  - **Edit ### xctl hooks section**: add paragraph 'When hooks fire': Portal fires resume hooks when pane freshly recreated from saved state on reboot recovery — i.e., tmux server started fresh and Portal restored sessions. Hooks do NOT fire on every detach/reattach within single server lifetime. If pane still exists, hook process either already ran or was explicitly killed; firing again would double-launch long-running processes like `claude --resume`. Deliberate.
  - **Edit ## Install** with ### Requirements subsection: tmux ≥ 3.0 (Feb 2020) — Portal uses array-indexed global hooks (set-hook -ga) requiring 3.0+. Earlier not supported; emits 'Portal requires tmux ≥ 3.0 (found <version>). Please upgrade.' and exits non-zero. Go, macOS, Linux unchanged.
  - **Edit ## Configuration** table: add state/ row with 'Saved session structure + scrollback for automatic restoration on reboot' purpose and PORTAL_STATE_DIR env. Note state/ contains sessions.json, scrollback files, daemon PID+version markers, portal.log. Link to Privacy Considerations.
  - **Edit ## Automatic Server Bootstrap** to ## Automatic Server Bootstrap & Restoration: Portal automatically starts tmux server if absent and restores saved sessions in same step. After reboot, sessions return with structure, layout, zoom, CWDs, scrollback (incl. ANSI). On any tmux-needing command, Portal checks server, starts if missing. Re-creates saved sessions not already live. Scrollback injected lazily on attach. Resume hooks fire on freshly-recreated panes. TUI shows 'Restoring sessions…' loading at most ~1.2s. CLI silent. Replaces tmux-continuum/tmux-resurrect for session persistence; uninstall those if installed (or disable @continuum-restore on) to avoid duplicate restoration.
- Add **### xctl state** subsection near ### xctl clean: portal state status — prints daemon status, last save, captured counts, state size, recent warnings. Non-zero exit if down/stale/warnings. portal state cleanup [--purge] — removes hooks + kills daemon. --purge wipes ~/.config/portal/state/.
- Do NOT add: exhaustive tmux API refs (spec non-goal); internal architecture diagrams (spec non-goal); changelog entries (release process); descriptions of internal subcommands (daemon/notify/signal-hydrate/hydrate/migrate-rename — internal).
- Run grep -n 'tmux-continuum|tmux-resurrect' README.md after edits to verify no stale references.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- ## Privacy Considerations exists covering 0600, local trust, no encryption, history-limit 0 / clear-history workarounds.
- ## Uninstall exists documenting both paths.
- ### xctl hooks includes hooks-fire-on-reboot-only paragraph.
- ## Install includes ### Requirements with tmux ≥ 3.0 + spec error message text.
- ## Configuration table includes state/ row with PORTAL_STATE_DIR.
- ### xctl state describes status + cleanup.
- ## Automatic Server Bootstrap rewritten to reflect Portal-owned restoration (renamed appropriately).
- No exhaustive tmux API refs.
- No architecture diagrams.
- No internal-command descriptions.
- No changelog entries.
- grep for 'tmux-continuum|tmux-resurrect' returns zero matches.
- Markdown renders correctly (lint via glow or GitHub preview).

**Tests**:
- README.md contains '## Privacy Considerations' heading
- README.md contains '## Uninstall' heading
- README.md mentions tmux ≥ 3.0 as requirement
- README.md xctl hooks section explains hooks-fire-on-reboot-only
- README.md configuration table includes state/ directory
- README.md no longer references tmux-continuum or tmux-resurrect as dependencies
- Manual review: read end-to-end, verify coherence and no orphan references

**Edge Cases**:
- Markdown link anchors: GitHub auto-generates lowercase-dashed anchors.
- Pre-feature Automatic Server Bootstrap section mentioned continuum/resurrect — rewriting removes that framing.
- Existing ### xctl hooks description NOT rewritten — augmented with firing-time paragraph.
- xctl vs portal state naming: README uses xctl consistently; state via xctl state.
- --purge documented in Uninstall with 'completely clean slate' warning.
- PORTAL_STATE_DIR env override (Phase 2 task 2-1) documented.
- Backward-compat: pre-feature Portal didn't restore. Post-upgrade users see new Restoration section. No explicit 'behaviour change' callout in README (changelog handles).
- Do NOT document internal subcommands (daemon/notify/signal-hydrate/hydrate/migrate-rename) — hidden from --help, absent from README.

**Context**: Spec 'Documentation Deliverables → README Privacy Considerations Section': v1 includes brief Privacy Considerations section covering scrollback persisted with mode 0600, same local-filesystem trust model as shell history, no encryption at rest, mitigations via history-limit 0 / clear-history. Exists because v1 ships without ephemeral-session opt-out (deferred per Scope). Spec 'README Uninstall Section': Two paths: just remove binary (defensive command -v portal handles residual tmux state); explicit teardown via portal state cleanup. Spec 'Existing User-Facing Documentation Updates': Hooks docs clarify fire on reboot recovery not every detach/reattach; Installation requirements document tmux ≥ 3.0; Storage location notes Portal writes to ~/.config/portal/state/ in addition to existing config files. Spec 'Not Documentation Scope': exhaustive tmux API refs, internal architecture diagrams, changelog entries.

**Spec Reference**: `.workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/specification/built-in-session-resurrection/specification.md` — section Documentation Deliverables.

title: Delete legacy bootstrap.NewShim and BootstrapDeps.Bootstrapper field
description: Phase 5 task 5-3 introduced cmd/bootstrap/shim.go's NewShim(ServerBootstrapper) Runner and the legacy BootstrapDeps.Bootstrapper field as a transitional adapter. This task deletes both after migrating remaining cmd-package test fixtures to inject the full bootstrap.Orchestrator via BootstrapDeps.Orchestrator. See .workflows/built-in-session-resurrection/planning/built-in-session-resurrection/phase-6-tasks.md task built-in-session-resurrection-6-12 for full Do/AC/Tests.
